{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01BknSd5xhyZFrtCCoo2qPhF","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"059ecfbe-2b86-4560-aee0-310048199391","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:00:21.962Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_014FoADDjSYWsuX3QczacPPi","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Browser Testing\n    32→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    33→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    34→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    35→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    36→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    37→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    38→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    39→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    40→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    41→\n    42→## Deployment\n    43→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    44→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    45→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (smart: skips rebuild when no new commits; `--force` to override)\n    46→- Deploy script compares HEAD before/after `git pull` — use `--force` when code was already pulled but not yet built\n    47→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    48→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    49→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    50→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    51→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    52→\n    53→## React + SQLite\n    54→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    55→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    56→\n    57→## Security\n    58→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    59→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    60→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    61→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    62→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    63→\n    64→## Working Directory\n    65→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    66→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    67→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    68→\n    69→## Security\n    70→- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n    71→- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n    72→- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n    73→- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n    74→- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n    75→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"5d1067a7-b8c7-4201-ac1d-ead3a7c819b2","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:00:23.183Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (smart: skips rebuild when no new commits; `--force` to override)\n- Deploy script compares HEAD before/after `git pull` — use `--force` when code was already pulled but not yet built\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n\n## Security\n- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n","numLines":75,"startLine":1,"totalLines":75}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Jux1hudciMh1sF9t38Au3j","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Registered Platforms\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub\n     4→- Repo: TommyBrennan/bookmate\n     5→- Project: Bookmate (#1)\n     6→- Auth: GH_TOKEN in .claude-beat/.env\n     7→\n     8→## Production Deployment\n     9→- **Host**: VPS at 89.167.127.85 (IPv6: 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1)\n    10→- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    11→- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx, ecosystem.config.js)\n    12→- **Port**: 3000 (direct, no reverse proxy)\n    13→- **Deploy**: `./scripts/deploy.sh`\n    14→- **No HTTPS**: No domain configured, no certbot/nginx (no root access)\n    15→- **DB**: SQLite at app/data/ (persistent on disk)\n    16→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"1a9bf738-e821-42c4-be75-47bd3670c3de","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:00:23.185Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/platforms.md","content":"# Registered Platforms\n\n## GitHub\n- Repo: TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- Project: Bookmate (#1)\n- Auth: GH_TOKEN in .claude-beat/.env\n\n## Production Deployment\n- **Host**: VPS at 89.167.127.85 (IPv6: 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1)\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx, ecosystem.config.js)\n- **Port**: 3000 (direct, no reverse proxy)\n- **Deploy**: `./scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **No HTTPS**: No domain configured, no certbot/nginx (no root access)\n- **DB**: SQLite at app/data/ (persistent on disk)\n","numLines":16,"startLine":1,"totalLines":16}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01WpucTyspvjw1wWFx6Sddw8","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-24 14:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Code review round 17: fixed 3 bugs (session destroy not awaited, PATCH body crash, BookSearch abort leak) and added middleware to block bot server action probes. Created issue #108, implemented fixes in PR #109, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 202 tests pass\n     8→- **CI**: Green on main\n     9→- **Open PRs**: None\n    10→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    11→- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n    12→- **PM2 logs**: Recurring \"Failed to find Server Action 'x'\" errors from bot probes — fixed with middleware\n    13→\n    14→## Work Completed\n    15→\n    16→### Issue #108: Code review round 17 (PR #109 — merged + deployed)\n    17→\n    18→4 fixes:\n    19→\n    20→1. **`session.destroy()` not awaited** (IMPORTANT) — Deleted user's cookie never actually cleared on client side because `destroy()` returns a Promise that wasn't awaited. Fixed with `await`.\n    21→\n    22→2. **PATCH /api/notifications crashes on missing body** (IMPORTANT) — `req.json()` threw unhandled SyntaxError when body is missing/malformed, producing 500 instead of 400. Fixed with try/catch wrapper. Added test.\n    23→\n    24→3. **BookSearch abort leak on early return** (IMPORTANT) — In-flight fetch not aborted when query shortened below 3 chars, causing potential state update on unmounted component. Fixed by aborting on early return path.\n    25→\n    26→4. **Middleware blocks server action probes** (HARDENING) — Bots sending `Next-Action: x` header caused repeated PM2 error logs. App uses zero server actions, so added middleware to return 400. Verified working in production.\n    27→\n    28→All 202 tests pass (1 new), ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n    29→\n    30→## Issues Monitored\n    31→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human, ~3 days remaining\n    32→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n    33→\n    34→## Next Session Priority\n    35→1. Check production health\n    36→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n    37→3. Consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n    38→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"555608af-5b98-4502-83ad-9413378cce9e","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:00:40.542Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-24-1400.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-24 14:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 17: fixed 3 bugs (session destroy not awaited, PATCH body crash, BookSearch abort leak) and added middleware to block bot server action probes. Created issue #108, implemented fixes in PR #109, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 202 tests pass\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n- **PM2 logs**: Recurring \"Failed to find Server Action 'x'\" errors from bot probes — fixed with middleware\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #108: Code review round 17 (PR #109 — merged + deployed)\n\n4 fixes:\n\n1. **`session.destroy()` not awaited** (IMPORTANT) — Deleted user's cookie never actually cleared on client side because `destroy()` returns a Promise that wasn't awaited. Fixed with `await`.\n\n2. **PATCH /api/notifications crashes on missing body** (IMPORTANT) — `req.json()` threw unhandled SyntaxError when body is missing/malformed, producing 500 instead of 400. Fixed with try/catch wrapper. Added test.\n\n3. **BookSearch abort leak on early return** (IMPORTANT) — In-flight fetch not aborted when query shortened below 3 chars, causing potential state update on unmounted component. Fixed by aborting on early return path.\n\n4. **Middleware blocks server action probes** (HARDENING) — Bots sending `Next-Action: x` header caused repeated PM2 error logs. App uses zero server actions, so added middleware to return 400. Verified working in production.\n\nAll 202 tests pass (1 new), ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human, ~3 days remaining\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider E2E testing, performance optimization, or new feature proposals\n","numLines":38,"startLine":1,"totalLines":38}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018qbR4yu3c7p11D7gFxWHSG","type":"tool_result","content":"title:\tAction Required: GitHub PAT expires in 7 days\nstate:\tOPEN\nauthor:\tTommyBrennan\nlabels:\tneeds-human\ncomments:\t4\nassignees:\t\nprojects:\tBookmate (Todo)\nmilestone:\t\nnumber:\t54\n--\n## What's needed\n\nThe GitHub personal access token (classic) **\"test-instance\"** used by the agent will **expire in ~7 days** (around March 27, 2026).\n\n## Why\n\nWithout a valid PAT, the agent cannot:\n- Push commits to the repository\n- Create/merge pull requests\n- Manage issues and project board\n- Run any `gh` CLI commands\n\n## How to fix\n\n1. Visit https://github.com/settings/tokens/3809420952/regenerate\n2. Generate a new token with the same scopes (delete:packages, notifications, project, repo, workflow, write:discussion, write:packages)\n3. Update the token in `.claude-beat/.env` on the server:\n   ```\n   GH_TOKEN=<new-token>\n   ```\n4. Update the git remote URL:\n   ```\n   git remote set-url origin https://<new-token>@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git\n   ```\n\n## Priority\n\n**High** — If not renewed before expiry, all agent operations will stop.","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"037b269c-889b-480f-a875-4c60f8c51f7e","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:00:58.027Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"title:\tAction Required: GitHub PAT expires in 7 days\nstate:\tOPEN\nauthor:\tTommyBrennan\nlabels:\tneeds-human\ncomments:\t4\nassignees:\t\nprojects:\tBookmate (Todo)\nmilestone:\t\nnumber:\t54\n--\n## What's needed\n\nThe GitHub personal access token (classic) **\"test-instance\"** used by the agent will **expire in ~7 days** (around March 27, 2026).\n\n## Why\n\nWithout a valid PAT, the agent cannot:\n- Push commits to the repository\n- Create/merge pull requests\n- Manage issues and project board\n- Run any `gh` CLI commands\n\n## How to fix\n\n1. Visit https://github.com/settings/tokens/3809420952/regenerate\n2. Generate a new token with the same scopes (delete:packages, notifications, project, repo, workflow, write:discussion, write:packages)\n3. Update the token in `.claude-beat/.env` on the server:\n   ```\n   GH_TOKEN=<new-token>\n   ```\n4. Update the git remote URL:\n   ```\n   git remote set-url origin https://<new-token>@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git\n   ```\n\n## Priority\n\n**High** — If not renewed before expiry, all agent operations will stop.","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Gf63rtsFGmapRA98LCctiT","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_019SFUdQnQX2zV8uSvA6GPeX","name":"Agent","input":{"subagent_type":"feature-dev:code-reviewer","description":"Code review round 18","prompt":"Do a thorough code review of this Next.js app at /root/Projects/bookmate/app. This is a book club matching platform using Next.js 15, SQLite (better-sqlite3), iron-session, Tailwind CSS v4.\n\nThis project has already had 17 rounds of code review, so look for subtle issues that may have been missed. Focus on:\n\n1. **API route edge cases** - missing validation, incorrect HTTP status codes, error handling gaps\n2. **Client-side bugs** - React state management issues, race conditions, memory leaks, incorrect useEffect dependencies\n3. **Security** - any remaining XSS vectors, auth bypasses, injection points\n4. **Data integrity** - SQL queries that could return wrong results, missing WHERE clauses, incorrect JOINs\n5. **Performance** - N+1 queries, unnecessary re-renders, missing indexes, large payloads\n6. **TypeScript** - unsafe type assertions, missing null checks, type narrowing issues\n\nFiles to examine (check all):\n- app/api/ (all API routes)\n- app/app/ (all pages and layouts)\n- app/components/ (all components)\n- app/lib/ (all library code)\n- app/middleware.ts\n\nReport only HIGH confidence issues with clear evidence. For each issue specify:\n- File path and line numbers\n- Severity (CRITICAL / IMPORTANT / MINOR)\n- Clear description of the bug/issue\n- Why it matters\n- Suggested fix\n\nDo NOT report:\n- Style/formatting issues\n- Issues that have already been fixed (look for try/catch, validation, etc.)\n- Theoretical issues without clear reproduction path\n- Things that are \"nice to have\" but work correctly as-is"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3836,"cache_read_input_tokens":20985,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3836},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"35121463-d719-4351-b301-76fda954aded"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_started","task_id":"ab182dbb352000e3d","tool_use_id":"toolu_019SFUdQnQX2zV8uSvA6GPeX","description":"Code review round 18","task_type":"local_agent","prompt":"Do a thorough code review of this Next.js app at /root/Projects/bookmate/app. This is a book club matching platform using Next.js 15, SQLite (better-sqlite3), iron-session, Tailwind CSS v4.\n\nThis project has already had 17 rounds of code review, so look for subtle issues that may have been missed. Focus on:\n\n1. **API route edge cases** - missing validation, incorrect HTTP status codes, error handling gaps\n2. **Client-side bugs** - React state management issues, race conditions, memory leaks, incorrect useEffect dependencies\n3. **Security** - any remaining XSS vectors, auth bypasses, injection points\n4. **Data integrity** - SQL queries that could return wrong results, missing WHERE clauses, incorrect JOINs\n5. **Performance** - N+1 queries, unnecessary re-renders, missing indexes, large payloads\n6. **TypeScript** - unsafe type assertions, missing null checks, type narrowing issues\n\nFiles to examine (check all):\n- app/api/ (all API routes)\n- app/app/ (all pages and layouts)\n- app/components/ (all components)\n- app/lib/ (all library code)\n- app/middleware.ts\n\nReport only HIGH confidence issues with clear evidence. For each issue specify:\n- File path and line numbers\n- Severity (CRITICAL / IMPORTANT / MINOR)\n- Clear description of the bug/issue\n- Why it matters\n- Suggested fix\n\nDo NOT report:\n- Style/formatting issues\n- Issues that have already been fixed (look for try/catch, validation, etc.)\n- Theoretical issues without clear reproduction path\n- Things that are \"nice to have\" but work correctly as-is","uuid":"03dc6fdb-5382-42f3-8c9a-9b08dfaf2b88","session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Do a thorough code review of this Next.js app at /root/Projects/bookmate/app. This is a book club matching platform using Next.js 15, SQLite (better-sqlite3), iron-session, Tailwind CSS v4.\n\nThis project has already had 17 rounds of code review, so look for subtle issues that may have been missed. Focus on:\n\n1. **API route edge cases** - missing validation, incorrect HTTP status codes, error handling gaps\n2. **Client-side bugs** - React state management issues, race conditions, memory leaks, incorrect useEffect dependencies\n3. **Security** - any remaining XSS vectors, auth bypasses, injection points\n4. **Data integrity** - SQL queries that could return wrong results, missing WHERE clauses, incorrect JOINs\n5. **Performance** - N+1 queries, unnecessary re-renders, missing indexes, large payloads\n6. **TypeScript** - unsafe type assertions, missing null checks, type narrowing issues\n\nFiles to examine (check all):\n- app/api/ (all API routes)\n- app/app/ (all pages and layouts)\n- app/components/ (all components)\n- app/lib/ (all library code)\n- app/middleware.ts\n\nReport only HIGH confidence issues with clear evidence. For each issue specify:\n- File path and line numbers\n- Severity (CRITICAL / IMPORTANT / MINOR)\n- Clear description of the bug/issue\n- Why it matters\n- Suggested fix\n\nDo NOT report:\n- Style/formatting issues\n- Issues that have already been fixed (look for try/catch, validation, etc.)\n- Theoretical issues without clear reproduction path\n- Things that are \"nice to have\" but work correctly as-is"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019SFUdQnQX2zV8uSvA6GPeX","session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"909f3a31-a308-407b-b50c-0db8256a1f1a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:01:34.653Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019KJf6NDcyRwXGbjs45zbAJ","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→{\n     2→  \"name\": \"bookmate\",\n     3→  \"version\": \"0.1.0\",\n     4→  \"private\": true,\n     5→  \"scripts\": {\n     6→    \"dev\": \"next dev --port 3000\",\n     7→    \"build\": \"next build\",\n     8→    \"start\": \"next start\",\n     9→    \"lint\": \"eslint . --max-warnings 50\",\n    10→    \"test\": \"vitest run\",\n    11→    \"test:watch\": \"vitest\"\n    12→  },\n    13→  \"dependencies\": {\n    14→    \"@tailwindcss/postcss\": \"^4.2.2\",\n    15→    \"@types/node\": \"^25.5.0\",\n    16→    \"@types/react\": \"^19.2.14\",\n    17→    \"bcryptjs\": \"^3.0.3\",\n    18→    \"better-sqlite3\": \"^12.8.0\",\n    19→    \"cookie\": \"^1.1.1\",\n    20→    \"iron-session\": \"^8.0.4\",\n    21→    \"next\": \"^15.5.14\",\n    22→    \"nodemailer\": \"^8.0.3\",\n    23→    \"postcss\": \"^8.5.8\",\n    24→    \"react\": \"^19.2.4\",\n    25→    \"react-dom\": \"^19.2.4\",\n    26→    \"tailwindcss\": \"^4.2.2\",\n    27→    \"typescript\": \"^5.9.3\"\n    28→  },\n    29→  \"devDependencies\": {\n    30→    \"@eslint/js\": \"^9.0.0\",\n    31→    \"@next/eslint-plugin-next\": \"^16.2.0\",\n    32→    \"@types/bcryptjs\": \"^2.4.6\",\n    33→    \"@types/better-sqlite3\": \"^7.6.13\",\n    34→    \"@types/nodemailer\": \"^7.0.11\",\n    35→    \"@vitest/coverage-v8\": \"^4.1.0\",\n    36→    \"eslint\": \"^9.39.4\",\n    37→    \"eslint-plugin-react-hooks\": \"^7.0.1\",\n    38→    \"typescript-eslint\": \"^8.57.1\",\n    39→    \"vitest\": \"^4.1.0\"\n    40→  }\n    41→}\n    42→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_0192AACSFb8Z7yobND4cRgq6","session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"9a33915f-af25-4200-862b-fed5f5405678","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:01:40.587Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01M3G8cMV89RwWWsE1S8QD3G","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→import fs from \"fs\";\n     4→\n     5→const DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\n     6→const DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n     7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01S382WLE7FobSCw1MRcX2RU","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { getIronSession, type SessionOptions } from \"iron-session\";\n     2→import { cookies } from \"next/headers\";\n     3→import db from \"./db\";\n     4→\n     5→export interface SessionData {\n     6→  userId?: number;\n     7→  email?: string;\n     8→  displayName?: string;\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→const DEV_SECRET =\n    12→  \"bookmate-development-secret-key-change-in-production-32chars!\";\n    13→\n    14→function getSessionOptions(): SessionOptions {\n    15→  const secret = process.env.SESSION_SECRET;\n    16→  if (!secret && process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\") {\n    17→    throw new Error(\n    18→      \"SESSION_SECRET environment variable is required in production\"\n    19→    );\n    20→  }\n    21→  return {\n    22→    password: secret || DEV_SECRET,\n    23→    cookieName: \"bookmate_session\",\n    24→    cookieOptions: {\n    25→      httpOnly: true,\n    26→      secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\" && process.env.HTTPS_ENABLED === \"true\",\n    27→      sameSite: \"lax\",\n    28→      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7, // 1 week\n    29→    },\n    30→  };\n    31→}\n    32→\n    33→export async function getSession() {\n    34→  const cookieStore = await cookies();\n    35→  return getIronSession<SessionData>(cookieStore, getSessionOptions());\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→export async function requireAuth() {\n    39→  const session = await getSession();\n    40→  if (!session.userId) {\n    41→    return null;\n    42→  }\n    43→  // Verify the user still exists in the DB — a deleted user's cookie\n    44→  // would otherwise grant full access until the session expires\n    45→  const user = db\n    46→    .prepare(\"SELECT id, display_name FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n    47→    .get(session.userId) as { id: number; display_name: string } | undefined;\n    48→  if (!user) {\n    49→    await session.destroy();\n    50→    return null;\n    51→  }\n    52→  // Keep session displayName in sync with DB (handles stale cookies)\n    53→  if (user.display_name !== session.displayName) {\n    54→    session.displayName = user.display_name;\n    55→    await session.save();\n    56→  }\n    57→  return session;\n    58→}\n    59→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019SFUdQnQX2zV8uSvA6GPeX","session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"cd0efd48-6616-4ce4-9add-32735e28d03b","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:01:43.953Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Q5c3j7SiCi6Lhpw1uiVtBu","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→\n     3→/**\n     4→ * Middleware to block invalid Server Action requests.\n     5→ *\n     6→ * This app uses zero Server Actions — all mutations go through API route handlers.\n     7→ * Any request with a `Next-Action` header is from a bot/scanner probing for\n     8→ * Next.js server actions. Returning 400 here prevents the framework from\n     9→ * logging noisy \"Failed to find Server Action\" errors in PM2 logs.\n    10→ */\n    11→export function middleware(req: NextRequest) {\n    12→  if (req.headers.get(\"next-action\")) {\n    13→    return NextResponse.json(\n    14→      { error: \"Server Actions are not supported\" },\n    15→      { status: 400 }\n    16→    );\n    17→  }\n    18→\n    19→  return NextResponse.next();\n    20→}\n    21→\n    22→export const config = {\n    23→  // Run on all routes except static assets and internal Next.js paths\n    24→  matcher: [\"/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico).*)\"],\n    25→};\n    26→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019SFUdQnQX2zV8uSvA6GPeX","session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"98b2ed84-02dd-4eb4-8848-48547133815d","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:01:44.000Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01WRwBx92PGyi974rGxLPsSR","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→\n     4→const PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n     5→\n     6→/** Escape LIKE metacharacters so user input is matched literally */\n     7→function escapeLike(value: string): string {\n     8→  return value.replace(/[%_\\\\]/g, \"\\\\$&\");\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n    12→  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n    13→\n    14→  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n    15→  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n    16→  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n    17→  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n    18→  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n    19→  const includePast = searchParams.get(\"include_past\") === \"true\";\n    20→  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n    21→  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : Math.min(pageParam, 1000);\n    22→\n    23→  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n    24→  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n    25→\n    26→  // Apply date floor: use the more restrictive of today or startDateFrom\n    27→  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    28→  const hasStartDateFilter = startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom);\n    29→\n    30→  if (!includePast) {\n    31→    // Use whichever is later: today or the explicit start_date_from filter\n    32→    const effectiveDate = hasStartDateFilter && startDateFrom > today ? startDateFrom : today;\n    33→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    34→    params.push(effectiveDate);\n    35→  } else if (hasStartDateFilter) {\n    36→    // includePast is true, but user wants a specific start date floor\n    37→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    38→    params.push(startDateFrom);\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    42→    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\' OR l.book_author LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\')\");\n    43→    params.push(`%${escapeLike(q)}%`, `%${escapeLike(q)}%`);\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    47→    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    48→    params.push(meetingFormat);\n    49→  }\n    50→\n    51→  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    52→    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\'\");\n    53→    params.push(`%${escapeLike(readingPace)}%`);\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n    57→\n    58→  const ORDER_MAP: Record<string, string> = {\n    59→    newest: \"l.created_at DESC\",\n    60→    oldest: \"l.created_at ASC\",\n    61→    start_date: \"l.start_date ASC\",\n    62→  };\n    63→  const orderClause = ORDER_MAP[sort] ?? \"l.created_at DESC\";\n    64→\n    65→  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n    66→\n    67→  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n    68→  const countResult = db\n    69→    .prepare(\n    70→      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    71→    )\n    72→    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n    73→\n    74→  const listings = db\n    75→    .prepare(\n    76→      `SELECT\n    77→        l.*,\n    78→        u.display_name as author_name,\n    79→        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    80→      FROM listings l\n    81→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    82→      WHERE ${whereClause}\n    83→      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n    84→      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    85→    )\n    86→    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n    87→\n    88→  return NextResponse.json({\n    89→    listings,\n    90→    pagination: {\n    91→      page,\n    92→      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n    93→      total: countResult.total,\n    94→      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    95→    },\n    96→  });\n    97→}\n    98→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019SFUdQnQX2zV8uSvA6GPeX","session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"a698fa3c-17c2-4616-92a2-6ff5d3810c83","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:01:47.508Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01ERejMtwPxQZzScb2DZZdo3","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { checkRateLimit } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     7→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     8→  if (!session) {\n     9→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    10→  }\n    11→\n    12→  // Rate limit: 10 listings per 15 minutes per user\n    13→  const rateLimitKey = `create-listing:${session.userId}`;\n    14→  const { allowed, retryAfter } = checkRateLimit(rateLimitKey, 10);\n    15→  if (!allowed) {\n    16→    return NextResponse.json(\n    17→      { error: \"Too many listings created. Please try again later.\" },\n    18→      { status: 429, headers: { \"Retry-After\": String(retryAfter) } }\n    19→    );\n    20→  }\n    21→\n    22→  try {\n    23→    const body = await req.json();\n    24→    const {\n    25→      bookTitle,\n    26→      bookAuthor,\n    27→      bookCoverUrl,\n    28→      bookOlid,\n    29→      language,\n    30→      readingPace,\n    31→      startDate,\n    32→      meetingFormat,\n    33→      maxGroupSize,\n    34→      requiresApproval,\n    35→      platformPreference,\n    36→    } = body;\n    37→\n    38→    if (!bookTitle || !bookAuthor || !readingPace || !startDate || !meetingFormat || !maxGroupSize) {\n    39→      return NextResponse.json(\n    40→        { error: \"Missing required fields\" },\n    41→        { status: 400 }\n    42→      );\n    43→    }\n    44→\n    45→    // Type check all string fields to prevent coercion bypasses\n    46→    if (typeof bookTitle !== \"string\" || typeof bookAuthor !== \"string\" ||\n    47→        typeof readingPace !== \"string\" || typeof startDate !== \"string\" ||\n    48→        typeof meetingFormat !== \"string\") {\n    49→      return NextResponse.json(\n    50→        { error: \"Invalid field types\" },\n    51→        { status: 400 }\n    52→      );\n    53→    }\n    54→\n    55→    if (typeof maxGroupSize !== \"number\" && typeof maxGroupSize !== \"string\") {\n    56→      return NextResponse.json(\n    57→        { error: \"Invalid group size\" },\n    58→        { status: 400 }\n    59→      );\n    60→    }\n    61→\n    62→    // Input length limits to prevent abuse\n    63→    if (bookTitle.length > 300 || bookAuthor.length > 200 || readingPace.length > 200) {\n    64→      return NextResponse.json(\n    65→        { error: \"One or more fields exceed maximum length\" },\n    66→        { status: 400 }\n    67→      );\n    68→    }\n    69→\n    70→    if (![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    71→      return NextResponse.json(\n    72→        { error: \"Invalid meeting format\" },\n    73→        { status: 400 }\n    74→      );\n    75→    }\n    76→\n    77→    if (!startDate || !/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n    78→      return NextResponse.json(\n    79→        { error: \"Invalid start date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)\" },\n    80→        { status: 400 }\n    81→      );\n    82→    }\n    83→\n    84→    const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    85→    if (startDate < today) {\n    86→      return NextResponse.json(\n    87→        { error: \"Start date cannot be in the past\" },\n    88→        { status: 400 }\n    89→      );\n    90→    }\n    91→\n    92→    const size = parseInt(String(maxGroupSize), 10);\n    93→    if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n    94→      return NextResponse.json(\n    95→        { error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" },\n    96→        { status: 400 }\n    97→      );\n    98→    }\n    99→\n   100→    const platform = [\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)\n   101→      ? platformPreference\n   102→      : \"telegram\";\n   103→\n   104→    // Validate book cover URL — only allow Open Library covers to prevent SSRF/tracking\n   105→    const sanitizedCoverUrl =\n   106→      bookCoverUrl && typeof bookCoverUrl === \"string\" &&\n   107→      bookCoverUrl.startsWith(\"https://covers.openlibrary.org/\")\n   108→        ? bookCoverUrl\n   109→        : \"\";\n   110→\n   111→    // Wrap listing creation + auto-join in a transaction for atomicity\n   112→    const createTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n   113→      const result = db\n   114→        .prepare(\n   115→          `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, book_cover_url, book_olid, language, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, requires_approval, platform_preference)\n   116→           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n   117→        )\n   118→        .run(\n   119→          session.userId,\n   120→          bookTitle.trim(),\n   121→          bookAuthor.trim(),\n   122→          sanitizedCoverUrl,\n   123→          bookOlid || \"\",\n   124→          language || \"English\",\n   125→          readingPace.trim(),\n   126→          startDate,\n   127→          meetingFormat,\n   128→          size,\n   129→          requiresApproval ? 1 : 0,\n   130→          platform\n   131→        );\n   132→\n   133→      // Auto-join the author as a member\n   134→      db.prepare(\n   135→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n   136→      ).run(result.lastInsertRowid, session.userId);\n   137→\n   138→      return result.lastInsertRowid;\n   139→    });\n   140→\n   141→    const listingId = createTransaction();\n   142→\n   143→    return NextResponse.json(\n   144→      { id: listingId },\n   145→      { status: 201 }\n   146→    );\n   147→  } catch (error) {\n   148→    console.error(\"Create listing error:\", error);\n   149→    return NextResponse.json(\n   150→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n   151→      { status: 500 }\n   152→    );\n   153→  }\n   154→}\n   155→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019SFUdQnQX2zV8uSvA6GPeX","session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"0dd66765-860b-4e2d-aac3-c56e18ffd9f0","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:01:47.837Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01H61ywyRopV7VqF9uPbR3od","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  // Author is already a member — don't allow re-joining\n    30→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer of this group\" }, { status: 400 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    37→      { status: 400 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    42→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    43→  }\n    44→\n    45→  // Check if already a member\n    46→  const existing = db\n    47→    .prepare(\n    48→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    49→    )\n    50→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    51→\n    52→  if (existing) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Check if user has a pending application (e.g., requires_approval was toggled off)\n    57→  const pendingApp = db\n    58→    .prepare(\n    59→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    60→    )\n    61→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    62→\n    63→  if (pendingApp) {\n    64→    return NextResponse.json(\n    65→      { error: \"You already have a pending application for this group\" },\n    66→      { status: 400 }\n    67→    );\n    68→  }\n    69→\n    70→  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n    71→  // Re-read max_group_size inside the transaction to avoid stale data from concurrent PATCH\n    72→  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    73→    const freshListing = db\n    74→      .prepare(\"SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    75→      .get(listingId) as { max_group_size: number } | undefined;\n    76→\n    77→    if (!freshListing) {\n    78→      return { error: \"Listing not found\", count: 0, maxSize: 0 };\n    79→    }\n    80→\n    81→    const { count } = db\n    82→      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    83→      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    84→\n    85→    if (count >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    86→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count, maxSize: freshListing.max_group_size };\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    db.prepare(\n    90→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    91→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    92→\n    93→    const newCount = count + 1;\n    94→    if (newCount >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    95→      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    96→    }\n    97→\n    98→    return { error: null, count: newCount, maxSize: freshListing.max_group_size };\n    99→  });\n   100→\n   101→  const result = joinTransaction();\n   102→\n   103→  if (result.error) {\n   104→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n   105→  }\n   106→\n   107→  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n   108→  // When group just filled, skip new_member notification for the author\n   109→  // since they'll receive the more important group_full notification instead\n   110→  if (result.count === result.maxSize) {\n   111→    notifyGroupFull(\n   112→      listingId,\n   113→      listing.book_title as string,\n   114→      listing.platform_preference as string\n   115→    );\n   116→  } else {\n   117→    notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n   118→  }\n   119→\n   120→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n   121→}\n   122→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019SFUdQnQX2zV8uSvA6GPeX","session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"9760dbaa-ff25-48d8-8fd4-918dd673817f","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:01:51.499Z"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JRpxjonyRQKkiZjUm2UNua","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { getSession, requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→import { isBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     6→import { isBotConfigured as isDiscordBotConfigured } from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     7→\n     8→export async function GET(\n     9→  _req: NextRequest,\n    10→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n    11→) {\n    12→  const { id } = await params;\n    13→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    14→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    15→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    16→  }\n    17→\n    18→  const listing = db\n    19→    .prepare(\n    20→      `SELECT\n    21→        l.*,\n    22→        u.display_name as author_name\n    23→      FROM listings l\n    24→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    25→      WHERE l.id = ?`\n    26→    )\n    27→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    28→\n    29→  if (!listing) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const members = db\n    34→    .prepare(\n    35→      `SELECT u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, lm.joined_at\n    36→       FROM listing_members lm\n    37→       JOIN users u ON lm.user_id = u.id\n    38→       WHERE lm.listing_id = ?\n    39→       ORDER BY lm.joined_at ASC`\n    40→    )\n    41→    .all(listingId) as { id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; joined_at: string }[];\n    42→\n    43→  const session = await getSession();\n    44→  const isMember = session.userId\n    45→    ? members.some((m) => m.id === session.userId)\n    46→    : false;\n    47→  const isAuthor = session.userId\n    48→    ? listing.author_id === session.userId\n    49→    : false;\n    50→\n    51→  // PRD: full listings should be invisible to non-participants\n    52→  if (listing.is_full && !isMember && !isAuthor) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json(\n    54→      { error: \"This listing is no longer available\" },\n    55→      { status: 404 }\n    56→    );\n    57→  }\n    58→\n    59→  // Check if the current user has an application (regardless of requires_approval,\n    60→  // since the flag may have been toggled after the application was submitted)\n    61→  let hasApplied = false;\n    62→  let applicationStatus = \"\";\n    63→  if (session.userId && !isMember) {\n    64→    const app = db\n    65→      .prepare(\n    66→        \"SELECT status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    67→      )\n    68→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { status: string } | undefined;\n    69→    if (app) {\n    70→      hasApplied = app.status === \"pending\";\n    71→      applicationStatus = app.status;\n    72→    }\n    73→  }\n    74→\n    75→  // Fetch pending applicants if the viewer is the author\n    76→  // Show regardless of requires_approval — pending apps can exist after the flag is toggled off\n    77→  let pendingApplicants: { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[] = [];\n    78→  if (isAuthor) {\n    79→    pendingApplicants = db\n    80→      .prepare(\n    81→        `SELECT la.id as application_id, u.id, u.display_name, u.bio, la.applied_at\n    82→         FROM listing_applications la\n    83→         JOIN users u ON la.user_id = u.id\n    84→         WHERE la.listing_id = ? AND la.status = 'pending'\n    85→         ORDER BY la.applied_at ASC`\n    86→      )\n    87→      .all(listingId) as { application_id: number; id: number; display_name: string; bio: string; applied_at: string }[];\n    88→  }\n    89→\n    90→  // Strip sensitive data from the response\n    91→  const safeListingData = { ...listing };\n    92→  // Always strip internal platform IDs — these are internal bot/channel identifiers\n    93→  delete safeListingData.telegram_chat_id;\n    94→  delete safeListingData.discord_channel_id;\n    95→  if (!isMember && !isAuthor) {\n    96→    // Strip invite links for non-members\n    97→    delete safeListingData.telegram_link;\n    98→    delete safeListingData.discord_link;\n    99→  }\n   100→\n   101→  return NextResponse.json({\n   102→    listing: {\n   103→      ...safeListingData,\n   104→      members,\n   105→      memberCount: members.length,\n   106→      isMember,\n   107→      isAuthor,\n   108→      hasApplied,\n   109→      applicationStatus,\n   110→      pendingApplicants,\n   111→      currentUserId: session.userId || null,\n   112→      telegramBotConfigured: isBotConfigured(),\n   113→      discordBotConfigured: isDiscordBotConfigured(),\n   114→    },\n   115→  });\n   116→}\n   117→\n   118→export async function PATCH(\n   119→  req: NextRequest,\n   120→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n   121→) {\n   122→  const session = await requireAuth();\n   123→  if (!session) {\n   124→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n   125→  }\n   126→\n   127→  const { id } = await params;\n   128→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n   129→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n   130→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n   131→  }\n   132→\n   133→  const listing = db\n   134→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   135→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   136→\n   137→  if (!listing) {\n   138→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n   139→  }\n   140→\n   141→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n   142→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the author can edit this listing\" }, { status: 403 });\n   143→  }\n   144→\n   145→  // Cannot edit if a platform link has been shared (group already formed)\n   146→  if (listing.telegram_link || listing.discord_link) {\n   147→    return NextResponse.json(\n   148→      { error: \"Cannot edit a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n   149→      { status: 400 }\n   150→    );\n   151→  }\n   152→\n   153→  try {\n   154→    const body = await req.json();\n   155→    const {\n   156→      language,\n   157→      readingPace,\n   158→      startDate,\n   159→      meetingFormat,\n   160→      maxGroupSize,\n   161→      requiresApproval,\n   162→      platformPreference,\n   163→    } = body;\n   164→\n   165→    // Validate fields that are provided — type check before string operations\n   166→    if (readingPace !== undefined && (typeof readingPace !== \"string\" || !readingPace || readingPace.length > 200)) {\n   167→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Reading pace is required (max 200 characters)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   168→    }\n   169→\n   170→    if (startDate !== undefined) {\n   171→      if (typeof startDate !== \"string\" || !/^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDate)) {\n   172→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid start date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   173→      }\n   174→      const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n   175→      if (startDate < today) {\n   176→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Start date cannot be in the past\" }, { status: 400 });\n   177→      }\n   178→    }\n   179→\n   180→    if (meetingFormat !== undefined && (typeof meetingFormat !== \"string\" || ![\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat))) {\n   181→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid meeting format\" }, { status: 400 });\n   182→    }\n   183→\n   184→    if (maxGroupSize !== undefined) {\n   185→      if (typeof maxGroupSize !== \"number\" && typeof maxGroupSize !== \"string\") {\n   186→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid group size type\" }, { status: 400 });\n   187→      }\n   188→      const size = parseInt(String(maxGroupSize), 10);\n   189→      if (isNaN(size) || size < 2 || size > 20) {\n   190→        return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Group size must be between 2 and 20\" }, { status: 400 });\n   191→      }\n   192→\n   193→      // Pre-check member count (authoritative check happens inside transaction below)\n   194→      const memberCount = (db\n   195→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   196→        .get(listingId) as { count: number }).count;\n   197→\n   198→      if (size < memberCount) {\n   199→        return NextResponse.json(\n   200→          { error: `Cannot reduce group size below current member count (${memberCount})` },\n   201→          { status: 400 }\n   202→        );\n   203→      }\n   204→    }\n   205→\n   206→    if (language !== undefined && (!language || typeof language !== \"string\" || language.length > 100)) {\n   207→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Language is required (max 100 characters)\" }, { status: 400 });\n   208→    }\n   209→\n   210→    if (platformPreference !== undefined && ![\"telegram\", \"discord\"].includes(platformPreference)) {\n   211→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid platform preference\" }, { status: 400 });\n   212→    }\n   213→\n   214→    // Build dynamic UPDATE\n   215→    const updates: string[] = [];\n   216→    const values: unknown[] = [];\n   217→\n   218→    if (language !== undefined) {\n   219→      updates.push(\"language = ?\");\n   220→      values.push(language);\n   221→    }\n   222→    if (readingPace !== undefined) {\n   223→      updates.push(\"reading_pace = ?\");\n   224→      values.push(readingPace.trim());\n   225→    }\n   226→    if (startDate !== undefined) {\n   227→      updates.push(\"start_date = ?\");\n   228→      values.push(startDate);\n   229→    }\n   230→    if (meetingFormat !== undefined) {\n   231→      updates.push(\"meeting_format = ?\");\n   232→      values.push(meetingFormat);\n   233→    }\n   234→    // Track whether maxGroupSize change needs is_full recalculation\n   235→    let needsIsFullRecalc = false;\n   236→    let parsedNewSize = 0;\n   237→    if (maxGroupSize !== undefined) {\n   238→      parsedNewSize = parseInt(String(maxGroupSize), 10);\n   239→      updates.push(\"max_group_size = ?\");\n   240→      values.push(parsedNewSize);\n   241→      needsIsFullRecalc = true;\n   242→    }\n   243→    if (requiresApproval !== undefined) {\n   244→      updates.push(\"requires_approval = ?\");\n   245→      values.push(requiresApproval ? 1 : 0);\n   246→    }\n   247→    if (platformPreference !== undefined) {\n   248→      updates.push(\"platform_preference = ?\");\n   249→      values.push(platformPreference);\n   250→    }\n   251→\n   252→    if (updates.length === 0) {\n   253→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"No fields to update\" }, { status: 400 });\n   254→    }\n   255→\n   256→    // Check if requires_approval is being toggled off — need to handle orphaned pending apps\n   257→    const togglingApprovalOff = requiresApproval === false && listing.requires_approval;\n   258→\n   259→    // Wrap in transaction to prevent race between is_full recalculation and concurrent joins\n   260→    // Use local copies of updates/values inside the transaction to avoid mutating outer arrays\n   261→    const updateTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n   262→      // Re-check platform links inside the transaction to prevent TOCTOU race:\n   263→      // a concurrent POST to /telegram or /discord could set a link between the\n   264→      // outer guard (line 146) and this point\n   265→      const current = db\n   266→        .prepare(\"SELECT telegram_link, discord_link FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   267→        .get(listingId) as { telegram_link: string | null; discord_link: string | null } | undefined;\n   268→      if (current?.telegram_link || current?.discord_link) {\n   269→        return { error: \"Cannot edit a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" };\n   270→      }\n   271→\n   272→      const txUpdates = [...updates];\n   273→      const txValues = [...values];\n   274→\n   275→      if (needsIsFullRecalc) {\n   276→        // Re-read member count and is_full inside transaction to prevent stale data\n   277→        const fresh = db.prepare(\"SELECT is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").get(listingId) as { is_full: number } | undefined;\n   278→        const currentMemberCount = (db\n   279→          .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   280→          .get(listingId) as { count: number }).count;\n   281→\n   282→        if (parsedNewSize < currentMemberCount) {\n   283→          return { error: `Cannot reduce group size below current member count (${currentMemberCount})` };\n   284→        }\n   285→\n   286→        if (parsedNewSize === currentMemberCount && !fresh?.is_full) {\n   287→          // Group is now exactly full — mark it\n   288→          txUpdates.push(\"is_full = ?\");\n   289→          txValues.push(1);\n   290→        } else if (parsedNewSize > currentMemberCount && fresh?.is_full) {\n   291→          // Group has room now — reopen it\n   292→          txUpdates.push(\"is_full = ?\");\n   293→          txValues.push(0);\n   294→        }\n   295→      }\n   296→\n   297→      txValues.push(listingId);\n   298→      db.prepare(`UPDATE listings SET ${txUpdates.join(\", \")} WHERE id = ?`).run(...txValues);\n   299→\n   300→      // When requires_approval is toggled off, auto-reject orphaned pending applications\n   301→      // so those users can join directly instead of being stuck in limbo\n   302→      if (togglingApprovalOff) {\n   303→        db.prepare(\n   304→          \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n   305→        ).run(listingId);\n   306→      }\n   307→\n   308→      return null;\n   309→    });\n   310→\n   311→    const txResult = updateTransaction();\n   312→    if (txResult?.error) {\n   313→      return NextResponse.json({ error: txResult.error }, { status: 400 });\n   314→    }\n   315→\n   316→    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n   317→  } catch (error) {\n   318→    console.error(\"Update listing error:\", error);\n   319→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n   320→  }\n   321→}\n   322→\n   323→export async function DELETE(\n   324→  _req: NextRequest,\n   325→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n   326→) {\n   327→  const session = await requireAuth();\n   328→  if (!session) {\n   329→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n   330→  }\n   331→\n   332→  const { id } = await params;\n   333→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n   334→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n   335→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n   336→  }\n   337→\n   338→  const listing = db\n   339→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   340→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   341→\n   342→  if (!listing) {\n   343→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n   344→  }\n   345→\n   346→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n   347→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the author can delete this listing\" }, { status: 403 });\n   348→  }\n   349→\n   350→  // Cannot delete if a platform link has been shared (initial check, re-verified inside transaction)\n   351→  if (listing.telegram_link || listing.discord_link) {\n   352→    return NextResponse.json(\n   353→      { error: \"Cannot delete a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n   354→      { status: 400 }\n   355→    );\n   356→  }\n   357→\n   358→  try {\n   359→    // Get members to notify (excluding the author)\n   360→    const members = db\n   361→      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id != ?\")\n   362→      .all(listingId, session.userId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   363→\n   364→    // Delete in a transaction — re-check link guard inside to prevent TOCTOU race\n   365→    const deleteTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n   366→      const current = db\n   367→        .prepare(\"SELECT telegram_link, discord_link FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   368→        .get(listingId) as { telegram_link: string | null; discord_link: string | null } | undefined;\n   369→\n   370→      if (current?.telegram_link || current?.discord_link) {\n   371→        throw new Error(\"LINK_SHARED\");\n   372→      }\n   373→\n   374→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   375→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   376→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   377→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM notifications WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   378→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM ratings WHERE listing_id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   379→      db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n   380→    });\n   381→\n   382→    try {\n   383→      deleteTransaction();\n   384→    } catch (txErr) {\n   385→      if (txErr instanceof Error && txErr.message === \"LINK_SHARED\") {\n   386→        return NextResponse.json(\n   387→          { error: \"Cannot delete a listing after the group chat link has been shared\" },\n   388→          { status: 400 }\n   389→        );\n   390→      }\n   391→      throw txErr;\n   392→    }\n   393→\n   394→    // Send deletion notifications after transaction (listing_id is NULL since listing is gone)\n   395→    for (const member of members) {\n   396→      try {\n   397→        createNotification(\n   398→          member.user_id,\n   399→          null,\n   400→          \"listing_deleted\",\n   401→          `The reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" has been cancelled by the organizer.`\n   402→        );\n   403→      } catch {\n   404→        // Notification failure should not block response\n   405→      }\n   406→    }\n   407→\n   408→    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });\n   409→  } catch (error) {\n   410→    console.error(\"Delete listing error:\", error);\n   411→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n   412→  }\n   413→}\n   414→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_019SFUdQnQX2zV8uSvA6GPeX","session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"8a0f672f-1d15-4578-b8a5-57fd9b87ee5a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:01:51.671Z"}
