{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_019SFUdQnQX2zV8uSvA6GPeX","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have enough information to write a thorough review. Let me compile the findings.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review — Bookmate App\n\nReviewing the full Next.js app at `/root/Projects/bookmate/app`. This is round 18 with a focus on subtle issues that survive earlier review rounds.\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL Issues\n\n### 1. `displayName` type not validated before string operations in registration — but more critically, `displayName` is not type-checked at all in `/api/auth/register`\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/auth/register/route.ts`, lines 9–48\n\n**Description:** `displayName` is destructured from the request body and immediately tested with `.length` (line 43) and `.trim()` (line 84) without any `typeof displayName === \"string\"` check. If a caller sends `{ \"displayName\": 12345 }`, the number passes the truthiness check on line 11, passes `.length > 100` (numbers have no `.length`, so this is `undefined > 100` = `false`), and then `.trim()` is called on a number, throwing a `TypeError` that falls through to the catch and returns a generic 500 instead of a 400.\n\nCompare this to `/api/profile/route.ts` line 14, which does correctly guard with `typeof displayName !== \"string\"`. The register route missed this pattern.\n\n**Why it matters:** Malformed requests return 500 instead of 400, which can confuse clients, pollute logs, and is a minor but real behavioral inconsistency.\n\n**Fix:** Add `if (typeof displayName !== \"string\") return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Display name must be a string\" }, { status: 400 });` before line 43, mirroring the profile route.\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n---\n\n### 2. `reject` action in `/api/listings/[id]/applications` runs outside a transaction — allows double-decision race\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`, lines 140–148\n\n**Description:** The `reject` branch (the `else` block at line 140) executes the `UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected'` statement directly outside any transaction and without a re-check of the current `status`. The application was already verified as `pending` at the outer query (line 44–55), but between that read and the write, a concurrent `approve` request could have already changed the status to `approved`. The rejection `UPDATE` would then silently overwrite an approved status with `rejected`, removing the user from consideration even though they were approved. The `approve` path correctly uses a transaction, but the `reject` path does not.\n\n**Why it matters:** With concurrent requests (e.g., organizer double-clicks), a user who was successfully approved can have their application rolled back to rejected. The `notifyApplicationDecision` on line 146 would then send a rejection email to an already-approved member.\n\n**Fix:** Wrap the reject update in an explicit transaction that re-reads `status = 'pending'` before writing:\n```ts\nconst updated = db.prepare(\n  \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n).run(applicationId);\nif (updated.changes === 0) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Application already decided\" }, { status: 409 });\n}\n```\n\n**Confidence: 90**\n\n---\n\n## IMPORTANT Issues\n\n### 3. `telegram_link` URL validation in client vs. server regex mismatch — server accepts paths server-side that client rejects\n\n**File (client):** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, line 259\n**File (server):** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/telegram/route.ts`, line 44\n\n**Description:** The client-side validation regex is `/^https:\\/\\/t\\.me\\/[\\w+/]+$/` (line 259), which requires at least one character after `https://t.me/`. The server-side check is simply `telegramLink.startsWith(\"https://t.me/\")` with a length cap. This means the server will accept `https://t.me/` (with nothing after the slash) because `startsWith` passes and the length is 16, well under 512. The client would block this, but a direct API caller would not. While a bare `https://t.me/` is not a functional invite link, it would be stored in the database and displayed to members as a broken link they cannot use.\n\n**Why it matters:** Members would see a non-functional Telegram link after the group fills.\n\n**Fix:** Add a stricter server-side pattern check: `!/^https:\\/\\/t\\.me\\/[\\w+/-]{1,490}$/.test(telegramLink)`.\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n---\n\n### 4. `notifyApplicationReceived` and `notifyGroupFull` called outside the transaction on the `apply` route — fired even on transaction rollback\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`, lines 103–104\n\n**Description:** `notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\")` is called unconditionally after `applyTransaction()` returns, but only if `result.error` is null. This path is correct for the success case. However, `better-sqlite3` transactions throw synchronously — if the transaction throws an unhandled exception (e.g., a DB constraint error not caught by the inner logic), the outer `try/catch` at the route level is absent here. There is no `try/catch` wrapping `applyTransaction()` in `apply/route.ts`. A DB-level `UNIQUE` constraint violation (e.g., race creating duplicate application) would throw, propagate past the `if (result.error)` check, and return a 500, but not trigger the notification — this is actually correct behavior. However, the route handler has no top-level `try/catch`, so uncaught exceptions from the transaction will propagate as unhandled Next.js errors rather than being returned as a clean 500.\n\n**Why it matters:** Unhandled exceptions in Next.js route handlers can cause the route to hang or return an unexpected response rather than a clean `{ error: \"Internal server error\" }`. Most other routes in this codebase wrap their logic in try/catch.\n\n**Fix:** Wrap the transaction call and the post-transaction notification call in a top-level `try/catch`:\n```ts\ntry {\n  const result = applyTransaction();\n  if (result.error) { ... }\n  notifyApplicationReceived(...);\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n} catch (error) {\n  console.error(\"Apply error:\", error);\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n}\n```\n\n**Confidence: 83**\n\n---\n\n### 5. `GET /api/listings` exposes `SELECT l.*` — leaks `telegram_link`, `discord_link`, and platform IDs to unauthenticated users browsing listings\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 74–86\n\n**Description:** The public listing browse endpoint uses `SELECT l.*`, which returns every column including `telegram_link`, `discord_link`, and `book_olid`. The individual listing GET (`/api/listings/[id]`) at line 91–99 carefully strips `telegram_chat_id`, `discord_channel_id`, and strips invite links for non-members. But the list endpoint does none of this. Any unauthenticated user can page through `/api/listings` and collect all Telegram/Discord invite links for groups that the listing author has already shared.\n\n**Why it matters:** The application design (lines 95–99 of the `[id]` route) explicitly intends invite links to be visible only to members. The list endpoint defeats this completely for any listing browsed while it is still open (before `is_full = 1` hides it). Even after a group fills, if a user bookmarked the listing URL while it was open, they would have had access to the link. More concretely: the list endpoint returns `telegram_link` and `discord_link` in the JSON for all open listings, which contradicts the member-only access intent.\n\n**Fix:** Replace `l.*` with an explicit column list that excludes `telegram_link`, `discord_link`, `telegram_chat_id`, `discord_channel_id`. Only fields needed for the card UI need to be returned.\n\n**Confidence: 88**\n\n---\n\n### 6. `userId` in `GET /api/profile/reputation` can be 0 or negative after `parseInt` of a crafted string\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reputation/route.ts`, lines 21–28\n\n**Description:** `userId` is computed as `parseInt(userIdParam, 10)`. The guard on line 24 checks `isNaN(userId)`, but `parseInt(\"0\", 10)` returns `0` (not NaN), and `parseInt(\"-5\", 10)` returns `-5` (not NaN). Both `0` and negative values pass the `isNaN` guard and are then used in the three DB queries. SQLite will simply find no records for a non-existent user ID and return zeroed stats rather than a 404 or 400. This is not a security issue, but it means the endpoint accepts `?userId=0` and returns a valid-looking JSON response (0 ratings, 0 groups), which is misleading and inconsistent with user IDs starting at 1 due to `AUTOINCREMENT`.\n\n**Fix:** Add `|| userId < 1` to the validation condition:\n```ts\nif (userIdParam && (isNaN(userId as number) || userId < 1)) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid userId parameter\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n---\n\n### 7. `ListingDetail.tsx` `fetchRatings` is called for non-member authors who are also members — but more critically, it is called without error handling for 403\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, lines 130–151\n\n**Description:** `fetchRatings` at line 133 calls `/api/ratings?listingId=${id}` without checking the authentication state. If the page loads while the user is unauthenticated (e.g., their session expired between the listing fetch and the rating fetch), the ratings API returns `{ error: \"Authentication required\", status: 401 }`. The `fetchRatings` function then checks `if (data.givenRatings)` at line 135 — this is falsy for the error response, so no crash occurs. However, `useEffect` at line 153 fires based on `listing?.is_full && listing?.isMember`. If `isMember` is true (server derived it from the session when the listing was fetched), but the session expires mid-page, ratings fetch silently fails with no feedback to the user. This is low-severity, but more importantly: the `fetchRatings` is also triggered any time `listing?.is_full` or `listing?.isMember` changes via `setListing`, which happens after every `handleJoin`, `handleApply`, and `handleDecision` call. This means ratings are re-fetched on every action, which is unnecessary.\n\n**Why it matters:** Unnecessary network requests on every state-changing action. Low severity but real performance waste.\n\n**Fix:** Move the `fetchRatings` call to only fire once after the listing is initially loaded (merge it into `fetchListing`), or add a `fetchedRatingsRef` similar to `fetchedTabs` in the profile page.\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n---\n\n## Summary Table\n\n| # | Severity | File | Issue |\n|---|----------|------|-------|\n| 1 | CRITICAL | `api/auth/register/route.ts:43` | `displayName` not type-checked before `.length` / `.trim()`, causes 500 on non-string input |\n| 2 | CRITICAL | `api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts:140` | `reject` path runs outside a transaction, allows concurrent `approve`+`reject` race to overwrite approved status |\n| 3 | IMPORTANT | `api/listings/[id]/telegram/route.ts:44` + `ListingDetail.tsx:259` | Server accepts `https://t.me/` (bare, no path), client validation is stricter; non-functional link stored |\n| 4 | IMPORTANT | `api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts` (no outer try/catch) | Missing top-level try/catch around transaction; DB exceptions propagate as unhandled errors |\n| 5 | IMPORTANT | `api/listings/route.ts:74` | `SELECT l.*` exposes `telegram_link` and `discord_link` to all public listing browse requests |\n| 6 | IMPORTANT | `api/profile/reputation/route.ts:24` | `userId=0` or negative passes `isNaN` guard, returns misleading zeroed response instead of 400 |\n| 7 | IMPORTANT | `ListingDetail.tsx:153` | `fetchRatings` re-triggered on every action that calls `setListing`, causing redundant API requests |\n\nThe most actionable fixes in order of impact: issue #5 (data exposure in list endpoint), issue #2 (application race), issue #1 (register type safety), then issues #3, #4, #6, #7."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: ab182dbb352000e3d (use SendMessage with to: 'ab182dbb352000e3d' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 95532\ntool_uses: 50\nduration_ms: 153539</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"0d7de0c3-4232-45e5-bf40-b28e2f1f76f1","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:04:08.192Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Do a thorough code review of this Next.js app at /root/Projects/bookmate/app. This is a book club matching platform using Next.js 15, SQLite (better-sqlite3), iron-session, Tailwind CSS v4.\n\nThis project has already had 17 rounds of code review, so look for subtle issues that may have been missed. Focus on:\n\n1. **API route edge cases** - missing validation, incorrect HTTP status codes, error handling gaps\n2. **Client-side bugs** - React state management issues, race conditions, memory leaks, incorrect useEffect dependencies\n3. **Security** - any remaining XSS vectors, auth bypasses, injection points\n4. **Data integrity** - SQL queries that could return wrong results, missing WHERE clauses, incorrect JOINs\n5. **Performance** - N+1 queries, unnecessary re-renders, missing indexes, large payloads\n6. **TypeScript** - unsafe type assertions, missing null checks, type narrowing issues\n\nFiles to examine (check all):\n- app/api/ (all API routes)\n- app/app/ (all pages and layouts)\n- app/components/ (all components)\n- app/lib/ (all library code)\n- app/middleware.ts\n\nReport only HIGH confidence issues with clear evidence. For each issue specify:\n- File path and line numbers\n- Severity (CRITICAL / IMPORTANT / MINOR)\n- Clear description of the bug/issue\n- Why it matters\n- Suggested fix\n\nDo NOT report:\n- Style/formatting issues\n- Issues that have already been fixed (look for try/catch, validation, etc.)\n- Theoretical issues without clear reproduction path\n- Things that are \"nice to have\" but work correctly as-is","agentId":"ab182dbb352000e3d","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I now have enough information to write a thorough review. If a caller sends `{ \"displayName\": 12345 }`, the number passes the truthiness check on line 11, passes `.length > 100` (numbers have no `.length`, so this is `undefined > 100` = `false`), and then `.trim()` is called on a number, throwing a `TypeError` that falls through to the catch and returns a generic 500 instead of a 400.\n\nCompare this to `/api/profile/route.ts` line 14, which does correctly guard with `typeof displayName !== \"string\"`. The register route missed this pattern.\n\n**Why it matters:** Malformed requests return 500 instead of 400, which can confuse clients, pollute logs, and is a minor but real behavioral inconsistency.\n\n**Fix:** Add `if (typeof displayName !== \"string\") return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Display name must be a string\" }, { status: 400 });` before line 43, mirroring the profile route.\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n---\n\n### 2. `reject` action in `/api/listings/[id]/applications` runs outside a transaction — allows double-decision race\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts`, lines 140–148\n\n**Description:** The `reject` branch (the `else` block at line 140) executes the `UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected'` statement directly outside any transaction and without a re-check of the current `status`. The application was already verified as `pending` at the outer query (line 44–55), but between that read and the write, a concurrent `approve` request could have already changed the status to `approved`. The rejection `UPDATE` would then silently overwrite an approved status with `rejected`, removing the user from consideration even though they were approved. The `approve` path correctly uses a transaction, but the `reject` path does not.\n\n**Why it matters:** With concurrent requests (e.g., organizer double-clicks), a user who was successfully approved can have their application rolled back to rejected. The `notifyApplicationDecision` on line 146 would then send a rejection email to an already-approved member.\n\n**Fix:** Wrap the reject update in an explicit transaction that re-reads `status = 'pending'` before writing:\n```ts\nconst updated = db.prepare(\n  \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n).run(applicationId);\nif (updated.changes === 0) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Application already decided\" }, { status: 409 });\n}\n```\n\n**Confidence: 90**\n\n---\n\n## IMPORTANT Issues\n\n### 3. `telegram_link` URL validation in client vs. server regex mismatch — server accepts paths server-side that client rejects\n\n**File (client):** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, line 259\n**File (server):** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/telegram/route.ts`, line 44\n\n**Description:** The client-side validation regex is `/^https:\\/\\/t\\.me\\/[\\w+/]+$/` (line 259), which requires at least one character after `https://t.me/`. The server-side check is simply `telegramLink.startsWith(\"https://t.me/\")` with a length cap. This means the server will accept `https://t.me/` (with nothing after the slash) because `startsWith` passes and the length is 16, well under 512. The client would block this, but a direct API caller would not. While a bare `https://t.me/` is not a functional invite link, it would be stored in the database and displayed to members as a broken link they cannot use.\n\n**Why it matters:** Members would see a non-functional Telegram link after the group fills.\n\n**Fix:** Add a stricter server-side pattern check: `!/^https:\\/\\/t\\.me\\/[\\w+/-]{1,490}$/.test(telegramLink)`.\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n---\n\n### 4. `notifyApplicationReceived` and `notifyGroupFull` called outside the transaction on the `apply` route — fired even on transaction rollback\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`, lines 103–104\n\n**Description:** `notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\")` is called unconditionally after `applyTransaction()` returns, but only if `result.error` is null. This path is correct for the success case. However, `better-sqlite3` transactions throw synchronously — if the transaction throws an unhandled exception (e.g., a DB constraint error not caught by the inner logic), the outer `try/catch` at the route level is absent here. There is no `try/catch` wrapping `applyTransaction()` in `apply/route.ts`. A DB-level `UNIQUE` constraint violation (e.g., race creating duplicate application) would throw, propagate past the `if (result.error)` check, and return a 500, but not trigger the notification — this is actually correct behavior. However, the route handler has no top-level `try/catch`, so uncaught exceptions from the transaction will propagate as unhandled Next.js errors rather than being returned as a clean 500.\n\n**Why it matters:** Unhandled exceptions in Next.js route handlers can cause the route to hang or return an unexpected response rather than a clean `{ error: \"Internal server error\" }`. Most other routes in this codebase wrap their logic in try/catch.\n\n**Fix:** Wrap the transaction call and the post-transaction notification call in a top-level `try/catch`:\n```ts\ntry {\n  const result = applyTransaction();\n  if (result.error) { ... }\n  notifyApplicationReceived(...);\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n} catch (error) {\n  console.error(\"Apply error:\", error);\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Internal server error\" }, { status: 500 });\n}\n```\n\n**Confidence: 83**\n\n---\n\n### 5. `GET /api/listings` exposes `SELECT l.*` — leaks `telegram_link`, `discord_link`, and platform IDs to unauthenticated users browsing listings\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts`, lines 74–86\n\n**Description:** The public listing browse endpoint uses `SELECT l.*`, which returns every column including `telegram_link`, `discord_link`, and `book_olid`. The individual listing GET (`/api/listings/[id]`) at line 91–99 carefully strips `telegram_chat_id`, `discord_channel_id`, and strips invite links for non-members. But the list endpoint does none of this. Any unauthenticated user can page through `/api/listings` and collect all Telegram/Discord invite links for groups that the listing author has already shared.\n\n**Why it matters:** The application design (lines 95–99 of the `[id]` route) explicitly intends invite links to be visible only to members. The list endpoint defeats this completely for any listing browsed while it is still open (before `is_full = 1` hides it). Even after a group fills, if a user bookmarked the listing URL while it was open, they would have had access to the link. More concretely: the list endpoint returns `telegram_link` and `discord_link` in the JSON for all open listings, which contradicts the member-only access intent.\n\n**Fix:** Replace `l.*` with an explicit column list that excludes `telegram_link`, `discord_link`, `telegram_chat_id`, `discord_channel_id`. Only fields needed for the card UI need to be returned.\n\n**Confidence: 88**\n\n---\n\n### 6. `userId` in `GET /api/profile/reputation` can be 0 or negative after `parseInt` of a crafted string\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reputation/route.ts`, lines 21–28\n\n**Description:** `userId` is computed as `parseInt(userIdParam, 10)`. The guard on line 24 checks `isNaN(userId)`, but `parseInt(\"0\", 10)` returns `0` (not NaN), and `parseInt(\"-5\", 10)` returns `-5` (not NaN). Both `0` and negative values pass the `isNaN` guard and are then used in the three DB queries. SQLite will simply find no records for a non-existent user ID and return zeroed stats rather than a 404 or 400. This is not a security issue, but it means the endpoint accepts `?userId=0` and returns a valid-looking JSON response (0 ratings, 0 groups), which is misleading and inconsistent with user IDs starting at 1 due to `AUTOINCREMENT`.\n\n**Fix:** Add `|| userId < 1` to the validation condition:\n```ts\nif (userIdParam && (isNaN(userId as number) || userId < 1)) {\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid userId parameter\" }, { status: 400 });\n}\n```\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n---\n\n### 7. `ListingDetail.tsx` `fetchRatings` is called for non-member authors who are also members — but more critically, it is called without error handling for 403\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, lines 130–151\n\n**Description:** `fetchRatings` at line 133 calls `/api/ratings?listingId=${id}` without checking the authentication state. If the page loads while the user is unauthenticated (e.g., their session expired between the listing fetch and the rating fetch), the ratings API returns `{ error: \"Authentication required\", status: 401 }`. The `fetchRatings` function then checks `if (data.givenRatings)` at line 135 — this is falsy for the error response, so no crash occurs. However, `useEffect` at line 153 fires based on `listing?.is_full && listing?.isMember`. If `isMember` is true (server derived it from the session when the listing was fetched), but the session expires mid-page, ratings fetch silently fails with no feedback to the user. This is low-severity, but more importantly: the `fetchRatings` is also triggered any time `listing?.is_full` or `listing?.isMember` changes via `setListing`, which happens after every `handleJoin`, `handleApply`, and `handleDecision` call. This means ratings are re-fetched on every action, which is unnecessary.\n\n**Why it matters:** Unnecessary network requests on every state-changing action. Low severity but real performance waste.\n\n**Fix:** Move the `fetchRatings` call to only fire once after the listing is initially loaded (merge it into `fetchListing`), or add a `fetchedRatingsRef` similar to `fetchedTabs` in the profile page.\n\n**Confidence: 80**\n\n---\n\n## Summary Table\n\n| # | Severity | File | Issue |\n|---|----------|------|-------|\n| 1 | CRITICAL | `api/auth/register/route.ts:43` | `displayName` not type-checked before `.length` / `.trim()`, causes 500 on non-string input |\n| 2 | CRITICAL | `api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts:140` | `reject` path runs outside a transaction, allows concurrent `approve`+`reject` race to overwrite approved status |\n| 3 | IMPORTANT | `api/listings/[id]/telegram/route.ts:44` + `ListingDetail.tsx:259` | Server accepts `https://t.me/` (bare, no path), client validation is stricter; non-functional link stored |\n| 4 | IMPORTANT | `api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts` (no outer try/catch) | Missing top-level try/catch around transaction; DB exceptions propagate as unhandled errors |\n| 5 | IMPORTANT | `api/listings/route.ts:74` | `SELECT l.*` exposes `telegram_link` and `discord_link` to all public listing browse requests |\n| 6 | IMPORTANT | `api/profile/reputation/route.ts:24` | `userId=0` or negative passes `isNaN` guard, returns misleading zeroed response instead of 400 |\n| 7 | IMPORTANT | `ListingDetail.tsx:153` | `fetchRatings` re-triggered on every action that calls `setListing`, causing redundant API requests |\n\nThe most actionable fixes in order of impact: issue #5 (data exposure in list endpoint), issue #2 (application race), issue #1 (register type safety), then issues #3, #4, #6, #7."}],"totalDurationMs":153539,"totalTokens":95532,"totalToolUseCount":50,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4267,"cache_read_input_tokens":87990,"output_tokens":3274,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4267},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DM5haEupH5GmcZMJMih4fh","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import bcrypt from \"bcryptjs\";\n     3→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     4→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     5→import { checkRateLimit } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n     6→\n     7→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     8→  try {\n     9→    const { email, password, displayName } = await req.json();\n    10→\n    11→    if (!email || !password || !displayName) {\n    12→      return NextResponse.json(\n    13→        { error: \"Email, password, and display name are required\" },\n    14→        { status: 400 }\n    15→      );\n    16→    }\n    17→\n    18→    // Rate limit: 3 registrations per IP per 15 minutes\n    19→    const ip = req.headers.get(\"x-forwarded-for\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() || \"unknown\";\n    20→    const { allowed, retryAfter } = checkRateLimit(`register:${ip}`, 3);\n    21→    if (!allowed) {\n    22→      return NextResponse.json(\n    23→        { error: \"Too many registration attempts. Please try again later.\" },\n    24→        { status: 429, headers: { \"Retry-After\": String(retryAfter) } }\n    25→      );\n    26→    }\n    27→\n    28→    if (password.length < 8) {\n    29→      return NextResponse.json(\n    30→        { error: \"Password must be at least 8 characters\" },\n    31→        { status: 400 }\n    32→      );\n    33→    }\n    34→\n    35→    // bcrypt only uses the first 72 bytes; cap to prevent DoS via slow hashing\n    36→    if (password.length > 72) {\n    37→      return NextResponse.json(\n    38→        { error: \"Password must be at most 72 characters\" },\n    39→        { status: 400 }\n    40→      );\n    41→    }\n    42→\n    43→    if (displayName.length > 100) {\n    44→      return NextResponse.json(\n    45→        { error: \"Display name must be at most 100 characters\" },\n    46→        { status: 400 }\n    47→      );\n    48→    }\n    49→\n    50→    if (email.length > 254) {\n    51→      return NextResponse.json(\n    52→        { error: \"Email address is too long\" },\n    53→        { status: 400 }\n    54→      );\n    55→    }\n    56→\n    57→    if (!/^[^\\s@]+@[^\\s@]+\\.[^\\s@]+$/.test(email.trim())) {\n    58→      return NextResponse.json(\n    59→        { error: \"Please provide a valid email address\" },\n    60→        { status: 400 }\n    61→      );\n    62→    }\n    63→\n    64→    // Normalize email before duplicate check (INSERT stores lowercase)\n    65→    const normalizedEmail = email.toLowerCase().trim();\n    66→\n    67→    // Check if email is already taken\n    68→    const existing = db\n    69→      .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?\")\n    70→      .get(normalizedEmail);\n    71→    if (existing) {\n    72→      return NextResponse.json(\n    73→        { error: \"An account with this email already exists\" },\n    74→        { status: 409 }\n    75→      );\n    76→    }\n    77→\n    78→    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(password, 12);\n    79→\n    80→    const result = db\n    81→      .prepare(\n    82→        \"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\"\n    83→      )\n    84→      .run(normalizedEmail, passwordHash, displayName.trim());\n    85→\n    86→    // Auto-login after registration\n    87→    const session = await getSession();\n    88→    session.userId = Number(result.lastInsertRowid);\n    89→    session.email = normalizedEmail;\n    90→    session.displayName = displayName.trim();\n    91→    await session.save();\n    92→\n    93→    return NextResponse.json(\n    94→      {\n    95→        user: {\n    96→          id: result.lastInsertRowid,\n    97→          email: normalizedEmail,\n    98→          displayName: displayName.trim(),\n    99→        },\n   100→      },\n   101→      { status: 201 }\n   102→    );\n   103→  } catch (error) {\n   104→    console.error(\"Registration error:\", error);\n   105→    return NextResponse.json(\n   106→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n   107→      { status: 500 }\n   108→    );\n   109→  }\n   110→}\n   111→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"4e2b105a-e201-462a-9a58-8748a1cdb13d","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:04:16.098Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/auth/register/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport bcrypt from \"bcryptjs\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { checkRateLimit } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  try {\n    const { email, password, displayName } = await req.json();\n\n    if (!email || !password || !displayName) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Email, password, and display name are required\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Rate limit: 3 registrations per IP per 15 minutes\n    const ip = req.headers.get(\"x-forwarded-for\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() || \"unknown\";\n    const { allowed, retryAfter } = checkRateLimit(`register:${ip}`, 3);\n    if (!allowed) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Too many registration attempts. Please try again later.\" },\n        { status: 429, headers: { \"Retry-After\": String(retryAfter) } }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (password.length < 8) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Password must be at least 8 characters\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    // bcrypt only uses the first 72 bytes; cap to prevent DoS via slow hashing\n    if (password.length > 72) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Password must be at most 72 characters\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (displayName.length > 100) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Display name must be at most 100 characters\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (email.length > 254) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Email address is too long\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    if (!/^[^\\s@]+@[^\\s@]+\\.[^\\s@]+$/.test(email.trim())) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Please provide a valid email address\" },\n        { status: 400 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Normalize email before duplicate check (INSERT stores lowercase)\n    const normalizedEmail = email.toLowerCase().trim();\n\n    // Check if email is already taken\n    const existing = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = ?\")\n      .get(normalizedEmail);\n    if (existing) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"An account with this email already exists\" },\n        { status: 409 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(password, 12);\n\n    const result = db\n      .prepare(\n        \"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\"\n      )\n      .run(normalizedEmail, passwordHash, displayName.trim());\n\n    // Auto-login after registration\n    const session = await getSession();\n    session.userId = Number(result.lastInsertRowid);\n    session.email = normalizedEmail;\n    session.displayName = displayName.trim();\n    await session.save();\n\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      {\n        user: {\n          id: result.lastInsertRowid,\n          email: normalizedEmail,\n          displayName: displayName.trim(),\n        },\n      },\n      { status: 201 }\n    );\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error(\"Registration error:\", error);\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n}\n","numLines":111,"startLine":1,"totalLines":111}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XZA31DxyEynfXKUWbYKp6p","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→\n     4→const PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n     5→\n     6→/** Escape LIKE metacharacters so user input is matched literally */\n     7→function escapeLike(value: string): string {\n     8→  return value.replace(/[%_\\\\]/g, \"\\\\$&\");\n     9→}\n    10→\n    11→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n    12→  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n    13→\n    14→  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n    15→  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n    16→  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n    17→  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n    18→  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n    19→  const includePast = searchParams.get(\"include_past\") === \"true\";\n    20→  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n    21→  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : Math.min(pageParam, 1000);\n    22→\n    23→  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n    24→  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n    25→\n    26→  // Apply date floor: use the more restrictive of today or startDateFrom\n    27→  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    28→  const hasStartDateFilter = startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom);\n    29→\n    30→  if (!includePast) {\n    31→    // Use whichever is later: today or the explicit start_date_from filter\n    32→    const effectiveDate = hasStartDateFilter && startDateFrom > today ? startDateFrom : today;\n    33→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    34→    params.push(effectiveDate);\n    35→  } else if (hasStartDateFilter) {\n    36→    // includePast is true, but user wants a specific start date floor\n    37→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    38→    params.push(startDateFrom);\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    42→    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\' OR l.book_author LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\')\");\n    43→    params.push(`%${escapeLike(q)}%`, `%${escapeLike(q)}%`);\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    47→    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    48→    params.push(meetingFormat);\n    49→  }\n    50→\n    51→  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    52→    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\'\");\n    53→    params.push(`%${escapeLike(readingPace)}%`);\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n    57→\n    58→  const ORDER_MAP: Record<string, string> = {\n    59→    newest: \"l.created_at DESC\",\n    60→    oldest: \"l.created_at ASC\",\n    61→    start_date: \"l.start_date ASC\",\n    62→  };\n    63→  const orderClause = ORDER_MAP[sort] ?? \"l.created_at DESC\";\n    64→\n    65→  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n    66→\n    67→  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n    68→  const countResult = db\n    69→    .prepare(\n    70→      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    71→    )\n    72→    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n    73→\n    74→  const listings = db\n    75→    .prepare(\n    76→      `SELECT\n    77→        l.*,\n    78→        u.display_name as author_name,\n    79→        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    80→      FROM listings l\n    81→      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n    82→      WHERE ${whereClause}\n    83→      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n    84→      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    85→    )\n    86→    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n    87→\n    88→  return NextResponse.json({\n    89→    listings,\n    90→    pagination: {\n    91→      page,\n    92→      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n    93→      total: countResult.total,\n    94→      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    95→    },\n    96→  });\n    97→}\n    98→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"8b3afe45-6ac2-4da8-8e6a-38049a07a1aa","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:04:16.100Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\n\nconst PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n\n/** Escape LIKE metacharacters so user input is matched literally */\nfunction escapeLike(value: string): string {\n  return value.replace(/[%_\\\\]/g, \"\\\\$&\");\n}\n\nexport async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n\n  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n  const includePast = searchParams.get(\"include_past\") === \"true\";\n  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : Math.min(pageParam, 1000);\n\n  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n\n  // Apply date floor: use the more restrictive of today or startDateFrom\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n  const hasStartDateFilter = startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom);\n\n  if (!includePast) {\n    // Use whichever is later: today or the explicit start_date_from filter\n    const effectiveDate = hasStartDateFilter && startDateFrom > today ? startDateFrom : today;\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(effectiveDate);\n  } else if (hasStartDateFilter) {\n    // includePast is true, but user wants a specific start date floor\n    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    params.push(startDateFrom);\n  }\n\n  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\' OR l.book_author LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\')\");\n    params.push(`%${escapeLike(q)}%`, `%${escapeLike(q)}%`);\n  }\n\n  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    params.push(meetingFormat);\n  }\n\n  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\\\'\");\n    params.push(`%${escapeLike(readingPace)}%`);\n  }\n\n  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n\n  const ORDER_MAP: Record<string, string> = {\n    newest: \"l.created_at DESC\",\n    oldest: \"l.created_at ASC\",\n    start_date: \"l.start_date ASC\",\n  };\n  const orderClause = ORDER_MAP[sort] ?? \"l.created_at DESC\";\n\n  const offset = (page - 1) * PAGE_SIZE;\n\n  // Get total count for pagination metadata\n  const countResult = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT COUNT(*) as total FROM listings l WHERE ${whereClause}`\n    )\n    .get(...params) as { total: number };\n\n  const listings = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT\n        l.*,\n        u.display_name as author_name,\n        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n      FROM listings l\n      JOIN users u ON l.author_id = u.id\n      WHERE ${whereClause}\n      ORDER BY ${orderClause}\n      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`\n    )\n    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    listings,\n    pagination: {\n      page,\n      pageSize: PAGE_SIZE,\n      total: countResult.total,\n      totalPages: Math.ceil(countResult.total / PAGE_SIZE),\n    },\n  });\n}\n","numLines":98,"startLine":1,"totalLines":98}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JDKHE1rknY8Ef6bWF15fHw","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationDecision, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function PATCH(\n     7→  req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the organizer can manage applications\" }, { status: 403 });\n    31→  }\n    32→\n    33→  const body = await req.json();\n    34→  const { applicationId, action } = body;\n    35→\n    36→  if (!applicationId || typeof applicationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(applicationId) || applicationId < 1 || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"Invalid request. Provide applicationId and action (approve/reject).\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  // Verify application belongs to this listing and is pending\n    44→  const application = db\n    45→    .prepare(\n    46→      \"SELECT * FROM listing_applications WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    47→    )\n    48→    .get(applicationId, listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    49→\n    50→  if (!application) {\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"Application not found or already decided\" },\n    53→      { status: 404 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  const applicantUserId = application.user_id as number;\n    58→\n    59→  if (action === \"approve\") {\n    60→    // Use transaction to ensure atomicity\n    61→    const approveTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    62→      // Re-read max_group_size inside transaction to avoid stale data from concurrent PATCH\n    63→      const freshListing = db\n    64→        .prepare(\"SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    65→        .get(listingId) as { max_group_size: number } | undefined;\n    66→\n    67→      if (!freshListing) {\n    68→        throw new Error(\"Listing not found\");\n    69→      }\n    70→\n    71→      // Check if group is already full\n    72→      const { count } = db\n    73→        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    74→        .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    75→\n    76→      if (count >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    77→        throw new Error(\"Group is already full\");\n    78→      }\n    79→\n    80→      // Update application status\n    81→      db.prepare(\n    82→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'approved', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    83→      ).run(applicationId);\n    84→\n    85→      // Add to members\n    86→      db.prepare(\n    87→        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    88→      ).run(listingId, applicantUserId);\n    89→\n    90→      // Check if group is now full\n    91→      const newCount = count + 1;\n    92→      let rejectedApps: { id: number; user_id: number }[] = [];\n    93→      if (newCount >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n    94→        db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    95→\n    96→        // Reject remaining pending applications inside the transaction\n    97→        rejectedApps = db\n    98→          .prepare(\n    99→            \"SELECT id, user_id FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n   100→          )\n   101→          .all(listingId) as { id: number; user_id: number }[];\n   102→\n   103→        for (const app of rejectedApps) {\n   104→          db.prepare(\n   105→            \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   106→          ).run(app.id);\n   107→        }\n   108→\n   109→        return { isFull: true, rejectedApps };\n   110→      }\n   111→      return { isFull: false, rejectedApps };\n   112→    });\n   113→\n   114→    try {\n   115→      const result = approveTransaction();\n   116→\n   117→      // Send notifications outside the transaction (non-critical)\n   118→      try {\n   119→        notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\");\n   120→\n   121→        if (result.isFull) {\n   122→          notifyGroupFull(\n   123→            listingId,\n   124→            listing.book_title as string,\n   125→            listing.platform_preference as string\n   126→          );\n   127→          for (const app of result.rejectedApps) {\n   128→            notifyApplicationDecision(app.user_id, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   129→          }\n   130→        }\n   131→      } catch (notifErr) {\n   132→        console.error(\"Notification error (approval succeeded):\", notifErr);\n   133→      }\n   134→\n   135→      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   136→    } catch (err) {\n   137→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Failed to approve\";\n   138→      return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n   139→    }\n   140→  } else {\n   141→    // Reject\n   142→    db.prepare(\n   143→      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n   144→    ).run(applicationId);\n   145→\n   146→    notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"rejected\");\n   147→\n   148→    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   149→  }\n   150→}\n   151→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"4be12d4f-6ed6-45bf-a516-e20b4ce72952","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:04:16.516Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/applications/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { notifyApplicationDecision, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\nexport async function PATCH(\n  req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Only the organizer can manage applications\" }, { status: 403 });\n  }\n\n  const body = await req.json();\n  const { applicationId, action } = body;\n\n  if (!applicationId || typeof applicationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(applicationId) || applicationId < 1 || ![\"approve\", \"reject\"].includes(action)) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Invalid request. Provide applicationId and action (approve/reject).\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Verify application belongs to this listing and is pending\n  const application = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT * FROM listing_applications WHERE id = ? AND listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    )\n    .get(applicationId, listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!application) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Application not found or already decided\" },\n      { status: 404 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const applicantUserId = application.user_id as number;\n\n  if (action === \"approve\") {\n    // Use transaction to ensure atomicity\n    const approveTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n      // Re-read max_group_size inside transaction to avoid stale data from concurrent PATCH\n      const freshListing = db\n        .prepare(\"SELECT max_group_size FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n        .get(listingId) as { max_group_size: number } | undefined;\n\n      if (!freshListing) {\n        throw new Error(\"Listing not found\");\n      }\n\n      // Check if group is already full\n      const { count } = db\n        .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n        .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n\n      if (count >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n        throw new Error(\"Group is already full\");\n      }\n\n      // Update application status\n      db.prepare(\n        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'approved', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n      ).run(applicationId);\n\n      // Add to members\n      db.prepare(\n        \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n      ).run(listingId, applicantUserId);\n\n      // Check if group is now full\n      const newCount = count + 1;\n      let rejectedApps: { id: number; user_id: number }[] = [];\n      if (newCount >= freshListing.max_group_size) {\n        db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n\n        // Reject remaining pending applications inside the transaction\n        rejectedApps = db\n          .prepare(\n            \"SELECT id, user_id FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n          )\n          .all(listingId) as { id: number; user_id: number }[];\n\n        for (const app of rejectedApps) {\n          db.prepare(\n            \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n          ).run(app.id);\n        }\n\n        return { isFull: true, rejectedApps };\n      }\n      return { isFull: false, rejectedApps };\n    });\n\n    try {\n      const result = approveTransaction();\n\n      // Send notifications outside the transaction (non-critical)\n      try {\n        notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"approved\");\n\n        if (result.isFull) {\n          notifyGroupFull(\n            listingId,\n            listing.book_title as string,\n            listing.platform_preference as string\n          );\n          for (const app of result.rejectedApps) {\n            notifyApplicationDecision(app.user_id, listingId, \"rejected\");\n          }\n        }\n      } catch (notifErr) {\n        console.error(\"Notification error (approval succeeded):\", notifErr);\n      }\n\n      return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    } catch (err) {\n      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : \"Failed to approve\";\n      return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n    }\n  } else {\n    // Reject\n    db.prepare(\n      \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'rejected', decided_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    ).run(applicationId);\n\n    notifyApplicationDecision(applicantUserId, listingId, \"rejected\");\n\n    return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n  }\n}\n","numLines":151,"startLine":1,"totalLines":151}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Hw2mzNrxVNjAMC1X9FMb4r","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationReceived } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json(\n    31→      { error: \"You cannot apply to your own listing\" },\n    32→      { status: 400 }\n    33→    );\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  if (!listing.requires_approval) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"This group does not require approval. Join directly.\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    44→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    45→  }\n    46→\n    47→  // Wrap membership check + application in a transaction to prevent TOCTOU races\n    48→  const applyTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    49→    // Check if already a member\n    50→    const isMember = db\n    51→      .prepare(\"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n    52→      .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    53→\n    54→    if (isMember) {\n    55→      return { error: \"You are already a member\", status: 400 };\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    // Re-check is_full inside transaction\n    59→    const currentListing = db\n    60→      .prepare(\"SELECT is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    61→      .get(listingId) as { is_full: number } | undefined;\n    62→\n    63→    if (currentListing?.is_full) {\n    64→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", status: 400 };\n    65→    }\n    66→\n    67→    // Check if already applied (pending)\n    68→    const existingApp = db\n    69→      .prepare(\n    70→        \"SELECT id, status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    71→      )\n    72→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { id: number; status: string } | undefined;\n    73→\n    74→    if (existingApp) {\n    75→      if (existingApp.status === \"pending\") {\n    76→        return { error: \"You have already applied\", status: 400 };\n    77→      }\n    78→      if (existingApp.status === \"approved\") {\n    79→        return { error: \"You are already approved\", status: 400 };\n    80→      }\n    81→      // If rejected, allow re-application by updating status back to pending\n    82→      db.prepare(\n    83→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'pending', decided_at = NULL, applied_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    84→      ).run(existingApp.id);\n    85→\n    86→      return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    // Insert new application\n    90→    db.prepare(\n    91→      \"INSERT INTO listing_applications (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    92→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    93→\n    94→    return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    95→  });\n    96→\n    97→  const result = applyTransaction();\n    98→\n    99→  if (result.error) {\n   100→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: result.status });\n   101→  }\n   102→\n   103→  // Notify outside transaction (fire-and-forget)\n   104→  notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n   105→\n   106→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   107→}\n   108→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"c27ff287-06ae-439f-89cf-c09957e402e5","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:04:16.999Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\nimport { notifyApplicationReceived } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\nexport async function POST(\n  _req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"You cannot apply to your own listing\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (!listing.requires_approval) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"This group does not require approval. Join directly.\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (listing.is_full) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Wrap membership check + application in a transaction to prevent TOCTOU races\n  const applyTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    // Check if already a member\n    const isMember = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n      .get(listingId, session.userId);\n\n    if (isMember) {\n      return { error: \"You are already a member\", status: 400 };\n    }\n\n    // Re-check is_full inside transaction\n    const currentListing = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n      .get(listingId) as { is_full: number } | undefined;\n\n    if (currentListing?.is_full) {\n      return { error: \"This group is already full\", status: 400 };\n    }\n\n    // Check if already applied (pending)\n    const existingApp = db\n      .prepare(\n        \"SELECT id, status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n      )\n      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { id: number; status: string } | undefined;\n\n    if (existingApp) {\n      if (existingApp.status === \"pending\") {\n        return { error: \"You have already applied\", status: 400 };\n      }\n      if (existingApp.status === \"approved\") {\n        return { error: \"You are already approved\", status: 400 };\n      }\n      // If rejected, allow re-application by updating status back to pending\n      db.prepare(\n        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'pending', decided_at = NULL, applied_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n      ).run(existingApp.id);\n\n      return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    }\n\n    // Insert new application\n    db.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO listing_applications (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n\n    return { error: null, status: 200 };\n  });\n\n  const result = applyTransaction();\n\n  if (result.error) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: result.status });\n  }\n\n  // Notify outside transaction (fire-and-forget)\n  notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n}\n","numLines":108,"startLine":1,"totalLines":108}}}

);\n    33→  }\n    34→\n    35→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    36→    return NextResponse.json(\n    37→      { error: \"Group must be full before sharing a platform link\" },\n    38→      { status: 58→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"1f967d30-ffaf-4e77-8636-ee89a24665a4","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:04:17.446Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/telegram/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\nexport async function POST(\n  req: NextRequest,\n  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  const { id } = await params;\n  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (listing.author_id !== session.userId) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Only the listing author can set the Telegram link\" },\n      { status: 403 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Group must be full before sharing a platform link\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const { telegramLink } = await req.json();\n\n  if (!telegramLink || !telegramLink.startsWith(\"https://t.me/\") || telegramLink.length > 512) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Please provide a valid Telegram invite link (https://t.me/..., max 512 characters)\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\n    telegramLink.trim(),\n    listingId\n  );\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n}\n","numLines":58,"startLine":1,"totalLines":58}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0168sazWDhB8Bo6fXG9cfKh5","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→/**\n     6→ * GET /api/profile/reputation?userId=X\n     7→ *\n     8→ * Returns a user's reputation score and rating breakdown.\n     9→ * If no userId is provided, returns the current user's reputation.\n    10→ */\n    11→export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {\n    12→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    13→  if (!session) {\n    14→    return NextResponse.json(\n    15→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    16→      { status: 401 }\n    17→    );\n    18→  }\n    19→\n    20→  const userIdParam = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"userId\");\n    21→  const userId = userIdParam ? parseInt(userIdParam, 10) : session.userId;\n    22→  const isOwnProfile = userId === session.userId;\n    23→\n    24→  if (userIdParam && isNaN(userId as number)) {\n    25→    return NextResponse.json(\n    26→      { error: \"Invalid userId parameter\" },\n    27→      { status: 400 }\n    28→    );\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  // Average score\n    32→  const stats = db\n    33→    .prepare(\n    34→      `SELECT\n    35→        COUNT(*) as total_ratings,\n    36→        ROUND(AVG(score), 1) as average_score,\n    37→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as five_star,\n    38→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as four_star,\n    39→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as three_star,\n    40→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as two_star,\n    41→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as one_star\n    42→       FROM ratings\n    43→       WHERE rated_user_id = ?`\n    44→    )\n    45→    .get(userId) as {\n    46→      total_ratings: number;\n    47→      average_score: number | null;\n    48→      five_star: number;\n    49→      four_star: number;\n    50→      three_star: number;\n    51→      two_star: number;\n    52→      one_star: number;\n    53→    };\n    54→\n    55→  // Number of completed groups\n    56→  const completedGroups = db\n    57→    .prepare(\n    58→      `SELECT COUNT(*) as count\n    59→       FROM listing_members lm\n    60→       JOIN listings l ON lm.listing_id = l.id\n    61→       WHERE lm.user_id = ? AND l.is_full = 1`\n    62→    )\n    63→    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n    64→\n    65→  // Number of groups where this user has given ratings\n    66→  const groupsRated = db\n    67→    .prepare(\n    68→      `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT listing_id) as count\n    69→       FROM ratings\n    70→       WHERE rater_id = ?`\n    71→    )\n    72→    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n    73→\n    74→  // Recent ratings received (with anonymized feedback)\n    75→  const recentRatings = db\n    76→    .prepare(\n    77→      `SELECT r.score, r.comment, r.created_at, l.book_title\n    78→       FROM ratings r\n    79→       JOIN listings l ON r.listing_id = l.id\n    80→       WHERE r.rated_user_id = ?\n    81→       ORDER BY r.created_at DESC\n    82→       LIMIT 10`\n    83→    )\n    84→    .all(userId) as {\n    85→      score: number;\n    86→      comment: string;\n    87→      created_at: string;\n    88→      book_title: string;\n    89→    }[];\n    90→\n    91→  // Strip comments from ratings when viewing other users' profiles (privacy)\n    92→  const sanitizedRatings = isOwnProfile\n    93→    ? recentRatings\n    94→    : recentRatings.map(({ score, created_at, book_title }) => ({\n    95→        score,\n    96→        created_at,\n    97→        book_title,\n    98→      }));\n    99→\n   100→  return NextResponse.json({\n   101→    reputation: {\n   102→      averageScore: stats.average_score || 0,\n   103→      totalRatings: stats.total_ratings,\n   104→      completedGroups: completedGroups.count,\n   105→      groupsRated: groupsRated.count,\n   106→      breakdown: {\n   107→        5: stats.five_star || 0,\n   108→        4: stats.four_star || 0,\n   109→        3: stats.three_star || 0,\n   110→        2: stats.two_star || 0,\n   111→        1: stats.one_star || 0,\n   112→      },\n   113→      recentRatings: sanitizedRatings,\n   114→    },\n   115→  });\n   116→}\n   117→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"0b9ac12b-f170-4ea0-9d6a-56cb2ab02b41","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:04:17.958Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reputation/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\n/**\n * GET /api/profile/reputation?userId=X\n *\n * Returns a user's reputation score and rating breakdown.\n * If no userId is provided, returns the current user's reputation.\n */\nexport async function GET(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n      { status: 401 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const userIdParam = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"userId\");\n  const userId = userIdParam ? parseInt(userIdParam, 10) : session.userId;\n  const isOwnProfile = userId === session.userId;\n\n  if (userIdParam && isNaN(userId as number)) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Invalid userId parameter\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Average score\n  const stats = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT\n        COUNT(*) as total_ratings,\n        ROUND(AVG(score), 1) as average_score,\n        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as five_star,\n        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as four_star,\n        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as three_star,\n        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as two_star,\n        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as one_star\n       FROM ratings\n       WHERE rated_user_id = ?`\n    )\n    .get(userId) as {\n      total_ratings: number;\n      average_score: number | null;\n      five_star: number;\n      four_star: number;\n      three_star: number;\n      two_star: number;\n      one_star: number;\n    };\n\n  // Number of completed groups\n  const completedGroups = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT COUNT(*) as count\n       FROM listing_members lm\n       JOIN listings l ON lm.listing_id = l.id\n       WHERE lm.user_id = ? AND l.is_full = 1`\n    )\n    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n\n  // Number of groups where this user has given ratings\n  const groupsRated = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT listing_id) as count\n       FROM ratings\n       WHERE rater_id = ?`\n    )\n    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n\n  // Recent ratings received (with anonymized feedback)\n  const recentRatings = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT r.score, r.comment, r.created_at, l.book_title\n       FROM ratings r\n       JOIN listings l ON r.listing_id = l.id\n       WHERE r.rated_user_id = ?\n       ORDER BY r.created_at DESC\n       LIMIT 10`\n    )\n    .all(userId) as {\n      score: number;\n      comment: string;\n      created_at: string;\n      book_title: string;\n    }[];\n\n  // Strip comments from ratings when viewing other users' profiles (privacy)\n  const sanitizedRatings = isOwnProfile\n    ? recentRatings\n    : recentRatings.map(({ score, created_at, book_title }) => ({\n        score,\n        created_at,\n        book_title,\n      }));\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    reputation: {\n      averageScore: stats.average_score || 0,\n      totalRatings: stats.total_ratings,\n      completedGroups: completedGroups.count,\n      groupsRated: groupsRated.count,\n      breakdown: {\n        5: stats.five_star || 0,\n        4: stats.four_star || 0,\n        3: stats.three_star || 0,\n        2: stats.two_star || 0,\n        1: stats.one_star || 0,\n      },\n      recentRatings: sanitizedRatings,\n    },\n  });\n}\n","numLines":117,"startLine":1,"totalLines":117}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01NzTgyRjtEd1uakjBTDdn4S","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Let me verify the issues:\n\n1. **Register route - displayName not type-checked**: Confirmed. Line 43 calls `displayName.length` without checking `typeof`. If `displayName` is a number, `.length` is `undefined` and `undefined > 100` is false, so it passes. Then line 84 calls `displayName.trim()` which throws TypeError on a number. The outer catch returns 500. Valid issue.\n\n2. **Reject path outside transaction**: Confirmed. Lines 140-148 - the reject path just runs the UPDATE directly without a transaction and without re-checking status='pending' in the WHERE clause. \n\nA concurrent approve could have already changed the status, so this is a race condition. Valid issue.\n\n3. **Telegram link validation**: Confirmed. Server only checks `startsWith(\"https://t.me/\")` but doesn't require any path after it. `https://t.me/` alone would pass. Minor but valid.\n\n4. **Apply route missing try/catch**: Looking at the code, the route doesn't have a top-level try/catch. If `applyTransaction()` throws rather than returning an error object, it would propagate unhandled. Since `better-sqlite3` transactions can throw on constraint violations, DB-level exceptions would bubble up. Valid issue.\n\n5. **SELECT l.* exposes links**: The list route uses `SELECT l.*` which includes all link fields, while the detail route carefully hides these for non-members. This is a real data leak and needs fixing first.\n\n6. **userId=0 or negative**: The guard only checks `isNaN`, so invalid IDs slip through. Minor issue.\n\nNow I'm prioritizing the fixes: the data exposure and race condition are critical, the type check and try/catch are medium priority, and the link validation and userId checks are lower priority. I should also check if the discord link route has the same telegram validation problem.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":10982,"cache_read_input_tokens":29375,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":10982},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"f1116a2a-1b21-47cc-a677-e6bd1fea17af"}
