 it(\"trims and lowercases email\", async () => {","newString":"  it(\"returns 400 when displayName is not a string\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/auth/register\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { email: \"a@b.com\", password: \"password123\", displayName: 12345 },\n    });\n\n    const response = await POST(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(400);\n    const data = await response.json();\n    expect(data.error).toContain(\"string\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 when email is not a string\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/auth/register\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { email: 123, password: \"password123\", displayName: \"User\" },\n    });\n\n    const response = await POST(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"trims and lowercases email\", async () => {","originalFile":"import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from \"vitest\";\nimport { createTestRequest, parseResponse } from \"../../helpers/mock-request\";\n\nconst testDb = vi.hoisted(() => {\n  const Db = require(\"better-sqlite3\");\n  const db = new Db(\":memory:\");\n  db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n  db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n      password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n      display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    );\n  `);\n  return db;\n});\n\nconst mockSession = vi.hoisted(() => ({\n  userId: undefined as number | undefined,\n  email: undefined as string | undefined,\n  displayName: undefined as string | undefined,\n  save: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\n  destroy: vi.fn(),\n}));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/db\", () => ({ default: testDb }));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/session\", () => ({\n  getSession: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(mockSession),\n  requireAuth: vi.fn(),\n}));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/rate-limit\", () => ({\n  checkRateLimit: vi.fn().mockReturnValue({ allowed: true, retryAfter: 0 }),\n}));\n\nvi.mock(\"bcryptjs\", () => ({\n  default: {\n    hash: vi.fn().mockResolvedValue(\"$2a$12$mockedhash\"),\n    compare: vi.fn(),\n  },\n}));\n\nconst { POST } = await import(\"@/app/api/auth/register/route\");\n\ndescribe(\"POST /api/auth/register\", () => {\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    vi.clearAllMocks();\n    mockSession.userId = undefined;\n    mockSession.email = undefined;\n    mockSession.displayName = undefined;\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"registers a new user successfully\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/auth/register\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { email: \"new@example.com\", password: \"password123\", displayName: \"New User\" },\n    });\n\n    const response = await POST(req);\n    const { status, data } = await parseResponse(response);\n\n    expect(status).toBe(201);\n    expect(data).toHaveProperty(\"user\");\n    expect((data as { user: { email: string } }).user.email).toBe(\"new@example.com\");\n\n    const user = testDb.prepare(\"SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ?\").get(\"new@example.com\");\n    expect(user).toBeTruthy();\n    expect(mockSession.save).toHaveBeenCalled();\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 when email is missing\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/auth/register\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { password: \"password123\", displayName: \"User\" },\n    });\n\n    const response = await POST(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 when password is too short\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/auth/register\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { email: \"a@b.com\", password: \"short\", displayName: \"User\" },\n    });\n\n    const response = await POST(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(400);\n    const data = await response.json();\n    expect(data.error).toContain(\"8 characters\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 when password exceeds 72 chars\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/auth/register\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { email: \"a@b.com\", password: \"a\".repeat(73), displayName: \"User\" },\n    });\n\n    const response = await POST(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 409 when email already exists\", async () => {\n    testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\").run(\n      \"taken@example.com\", \"$2a$12$hash\", \"Existing\"\n    );\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/auth/register\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { email: \"taken@example.com\", password: \"password123\", displayName: \"Another\" },\n    });\n\n    const response = await POST(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(409);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 when display name is too long\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/auth/register\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { email: \"a@b.com\", password: \"password123\", displayName: \"A\".repeat(101) },\n    });\n\n    const response = await POST(req);\n    expect(response.status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"trims and lowercases email\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/auth/register\", {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      body: { email: \"  User@Example.COM  \", password: \"password123\", displayName: \"User\" },\n    });\n\n    const response = await POST(req);\n    const data = await response.json();\n    expect(data.user.email).toBe(\"user@example.com\");\n  });\n});\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":130,"oldLines":6,"newStart":130,"newLines":28,"lines":["     expect(response.status).toBe(400);"," {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01NGLSwYhkTu7xPdJHyLC1aK","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Wbh7ihHAcaU2vCXB7eaevn","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/__tests__/api/listings/listings.test.ts","old_string":"  it(\"returns listings sorted newest first by default (by id descending)\", async () => {","new_string":"  it(\"does not expose telegram_link or discord_link in listing list\", async () => {\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId);\n    testDb.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ?, discord_link = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\n      \"https://t.me/secret\", \"https://discord.gg/secret\", listingId\n    );\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/listings\");\n    const response = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<{ listings: Record<string, unknown>[] }>(response);\n\n    expect(data.listings.length).toBe(1);\n    expect(data.listings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"telegram_link\");\n    expect(data.listings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"discord_link\");\n    expect(data.listings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"telegram_chat_id\");\n    expect(data.listings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"discord_channel_id\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns listings sorted newest first by default (by id descending)\", async () => {"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":440,"cache_read_input_tokens":54863,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":440},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"a256a13d-be61-4038-8cc8-411eda60c675"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Wbh7ihHAcaU2vCXB7eaevn","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/__tests__/api/listings/listings.test.ts has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"b9c61d4c-5e2a-4654-889c-c381c75dd881","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:07:11.978Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/__tests__/api/listings/listings.test.ts","oldString":"  it(\"returns listings sorted newest first by default (by id descending)\", async () => {","newString":"  it(\"does not expose telegram_link or discord_link in listing list\", async () => {\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId);\n    testDb.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ?, discord_link = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\n      \"https://t.me/secret\", \"https://discord.gg/secret\", listingId\n    );\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/listings\");\n    const response = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<{ listings: Record<string, unknown>[] }>(response);\n\n    expect(data.listings.length).toBe(1);\n    expect(data.listings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"telegram_link\");\n    expect(data.listings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"discord_link\");\n    expect(data.listings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"telegram_chat_id\");\n    expect(data.listings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"discord_channel_id\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns listings sorted newest first by default (by id descending)\", async () => {","originalFile":"import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from \"vitest\";\nimport { createTestRequest, parseResponse } from \"../../helpers/mock-request\";\n\nconst testDb = vi.hoisted(() => {\n  const Db = require(\"better-sqlite3\");\n  const db = new Db(\":memory:\");\n  db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n  db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n      password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n      display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n      reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n      start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n      meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n      max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n      telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram',\n      discord_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      telegram_chat_id INTEGER,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n      listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n  `);\n  return db;\n});\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/db\", () => ({ default: testDb }));\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/session\", () => ({\n  getSession: vi.fn(),\n  requireAuth: vi.fn(),\n}));\n\nconst { GET } = await import(\"@/app/api/listings/route\");\n\nfunction insertUser(email = \"test@example.com\", name = \"Test User\") {\n  testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\").run(email, \"hash\", name);\n  return testDb.prepare(\"SELECT last_insert_rowid() as id\").get().id as number;\n}\n\nfunction insertListing(\n  authorId: number,\n  overrides: Partial<{\n    book_title: string;\n    book_author: string;\n    meeting_format: string;\n    is_full: number;\n  }> = {}\n) {\n  testDb.prepare(\n    `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, is_full)\n     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n  ).run(\n    authorId,\n    overrides.book_title ?? \"Test Book\",\n    overrides.book_author ?? \"Test Author\",\n    \"1ch/wk\",\n    \"2026-04-01\",\n    overrides.meeting_format ?? \"text\",\n    5,\n    overrides.is_full ?? 0\n  );\n  return testDb.prepare(\"SELECT last_insert_rowid() as id\").get().id as number;\n}\n\ndescribe(\"GET /api/listings\", () => {\n  let userId: number;\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listing_members\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");\n    userId = insertUser();\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns empty array when no listings exist\", async () => {\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/listings\");\n    const response = await GET(req);\n    const { status, data } = await parseResponse<{ listings: unknown[]; pagination: { page: number; pageSize: number; total: number; totalPages: number } }>(response);\n\n    expect(status).toBe(200);\n    expect(data.listings).toEqual([]);\n    expect(data.pagination).toEqual({\n      page: 1,\n      pageSize: 20,\n      total: 0,\n      totalPages: 0,\n    });\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns open listings only (hides full listings)\", async () => {\n    insertListing(userId, { book_title: \"Open Book\" });\n    insertListing(userId, { book_title: \"Full Book\", is_full: 1 });\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/listings\");\n    const response = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<{ listings: { book_title: string }[] }>(response);\n\n    expect(data.listings.length).toBe(1);\n    expect(data.listings[0].book_title).toBe(\"Open Book\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"includes member count in response\", async () => {\n    const listingId = insertListing(userId);\n    testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\").run(listingId, userId);\n\n    const user2 = insertUser(\"user2@example.com\", \"User 2\");\n    testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\").run(listingId, user2);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/listings\");\n    const response = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<{ listings: { member_count: number }[] }>(response);\n\n    expect(data.listings[0].member_count).toBe(2);\n  });\n\n  // Route uses \"q\" param for search, not \"search\"\n  it(\"supports search by book title via q param\", async () => {\n    insertListing(userId, { book_title: \"The Great Gatsby\" });\n    insertListing(userId, { book_title: \"1984\" });\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/listings?q=gatsby\");\n    const response = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<{ listings: { book_title: string }[] }>(response);\n\n    expect(data.listings.length).toBe(1);\n    expect(data.listings[0].book_title).toBe(\"The Great Gatsby\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"supports search by book author via q param\", async () => {\n    insertListing(userId, { book_title: \"Book A\", book_author: \"Tolkien\" });\n    insertListing(userId, { book_title: \"Book B\", book_author: \"Rowling\" });\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/listings?q=tolkien\");\n    const response = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<{ listings: { book_author: string }[] }>(response);\n\n    expect(data.listings.length).toBe(1);\n    expect(data.listings[0].book_author).toBe(\"Tolkien\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"supports filtering by meeting_format\", async () => {\n    insertListing(userId, { meeting_format: \"voice\" });\n    insertListing(userId, { meeting_format: \"text\" });\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/listings?meeting_format=voice\");\n    const response = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<{ listings: unknown[] }>(response);\n\n    expect(data.listings.length).toBe(1);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns listings sorted newest first by default (by id descending)\", async () => {\n    const id1 = insertListing(userId, { book_title: \"First\" });\n    const id2 = insertListing(userId, { book_title: \"Second\" });\n\n    // Manually set different created_at timestamps to ensure sort order\n    testDb.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET created_at = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\"2026-03-01 00:00:00\", id1);\n    testDb.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET created_at = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(\"2026-03-02 00:00:00\", id2);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/listings\");\n    const response = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<{ listings: { book_title: string }[] }>(response);\n\n    expect(data.listings[0].book_title).toBe(\"Second\");\n    expect(data.listings[1].book_title).toBe(\"First\");\n  });\n});\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":175,"oldLines":6,"newStart":175,"newLines":23,"lines":["     expect(data.listings.length).toBe(1);","   });"," ","+  it(\"does not expose telegram_link or discord_link in listing list\", async () => {","+    const listingId = insertListing(userId);","+    testDb.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ?, discord_link = ? WHERE id = ?\").run(","+      \"https://t.me/secret\", \"https://discord.gg/secret\", listingId","+    );","+","+    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/listings\");","+    const response = await GET(req);","+    const { data } = await parseResponse<{ listings: Record<string, unknown>[] }>(response);","+","+    expect(data.listings.length).toBe(1);","+    expect(data.listings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"telegram_link\");","+    expect(data.listings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"discord_link\");","+    expect(data.listings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"telegram_chat_id\");","+    expect(data.listings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"discord_channel_id\");","+  });","+","   it(\"returns listings sorted newest first by default (by id descending)\", async () => {","     const id1 = insertListing(userId, { book_title: \"First\" });","     const id2 = insertListing(userId, { book_title: \"Second\" });"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

);\n    48→    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n    49→      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    50→      listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→      rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    52→ { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from \"vitest\";\nimport { createTestRequest, parseResponse } from \"../../helpers/mock-request\";\n\nconst testDb = vi.hoisted(() => {\n  const Db = require(\"better-sqlite3\");\n  const db = new Db(\":memory:\");\n  db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n  db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n      password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n      display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n      reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n      start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n      meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n      max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n      telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram',\n      discord_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      telegram_chat_id INTEGER,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n      listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      rated_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      score INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(score >= 1 AND score <= 5),\n      comment TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      UNIQUE(listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (rater_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (rated_user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n  `);\n  return db;\n});\n\nconst mockSession = vi.hoisted(() => ({\n  userId: 1 as number | undefined,\n}));\n\nconst mockRequireAuth = vi.hoisted(() => vi.fn());\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/db\", () => ({ default: testDb }));\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/session\", () => ({ requireAuth: mockRequireAuth }));\n\nconst { GET } = await import(\"@/app/api/profile/reputation/route\");\n\ninterface ReputationData {\n  reputation: {\n    averageScore: number;\n    totalRatings: number;\n    completedGroups: number;\n    groupsRated: number;\n    breakdown: Record<number, number>;\n    recentRatings: Array<{\n      score: number;\n      created_at: string;\n      book_title: string;\n      comment?: string;\n    }>;\n  };\n}\n\ndescribe(\"GET /api/profile/reputation\", () => {\n  let user2Id: number;\n  let listingId: number;\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    vi.clearAllMocks();\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM ratings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listing_members\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":212}}}

INTO ratings (listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id, score, comment) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n   184→    ).run(listingId, 1, user2Id, 3, \"Secret feedback\");\n   185→\n   186→    const req = expect(data.reputation.recentRatings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"comment\");\n   190→  });\n   191→\n   192→  it(\"returns 400 for invalid userId parameter\", async () => {\n   193→    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO users (id, email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, \"user1@test.com\", \"hash\", \"User One\");\n\n    const result = testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(\"user2@test.com\", \"hash\", \"User Two\");\n    user2Id = Number(result.lastInsertRowid);\n\n    const listingResult = testDb.prepare(\n      `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, is_full)\n       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n    ).run(1, \"Test Book\", \"Author\", \"1 ch/week\", \"2026-01-01\", \"text\", 5, 1);\n    listingId = Number(listingResult.lastInsertRowid);\n\n    testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\").run(listingId, 1);\n    testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\").run(listingId, user2Id);\n\n    mockSession.userId = 1;\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 401 when not authenticated\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(null);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    expect(res.status).toBe(401);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns zero reputation for user with no ratings\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<ReputationData>(res);\n    expect(data.reputation.averageScore).toBe(0);\n    expect(data.reputation.totalRatings).toBe(0);\n    expect(data.reputation.recentRatings).toEqual([]);\n  });\n\n  it(\"calculates average score and breakdown correctly\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n\n    // User2 rates User1 with score 5\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO ratings (listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id, score, comment) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(listingId, user2Id, 1, 5, \"Great reader!\");\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<ReputationData>(res);\n    expect(data.reputation.averageScore).toBe(5);\n    expect(data.reputation.totalRatings).toBe(1);\n    expect(data.reputation.breakdown[5]).toBe(1);\n    expect(data.reputation.breakdown[4]).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  it(\"counts completed groups correctly\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<ReputationData>(res);\n    // User 1 is member of 1 full listing\n    expect(data.reputation.completedGroups).toBe(1);\n  });\n\n  it(\"includes comments for own profile\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO ratings (listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id, score, comment) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(listingId, user2Id, 1, 4, \"Insightful discussions\");\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<ReputationData>(res);\n    expect(data.reputation.recentRatings[0].comment).toBe(\"Insightful discussions\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"strips comments when viewing another user's profile\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    // Rate user2\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO ratings (listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id, score, comment) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(listingId, 1, user2Id, 3, \"Secret feedback\");\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(`/api/profile/reputation?userId=${user2Id}`);\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<ReputationData>(res);\n    expect(data.reputation.recentRatings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"comment\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 for invalid userId parameter\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation?userId=abc\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    expect(res.status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"counts groups rated by user\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    // User1 rates User2\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO ratings (listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id, score) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(listingId, 1, user2Id, 4);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<ReputationData>(res);\n    expect(data.reputation.groupsRated).toBe(1);\n  });\n});\n","numLines":113,"startLine":100,"totalLines":212}}}

 it(\"returns 400 for invalid userId parameter\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation?userId=abc\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    expect(res.status).toBe(400);\n  });","newString":"  it(\"returns 400 for invalid userId parameter\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation?userId=abc\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    expect(res.status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 for userId=0\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation?userId=0\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    expect(res.status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 for negative userId\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation?userId=-5\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    expect(res.status).toBe(400);\n  });","originalFile":"import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from \"vitest\";\nimport { createTestRequest, parseResponse } from \"../../helpers/mock-request\";\n\nconst testDb = vi.hoisted(() => {\n  const Db = require(\"better-sqlite3\");\n  const db = new Db(\":memory:\");\n  db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n  db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n      password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n      display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n      book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n      reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n      start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n      meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n      max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n      telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n      platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram',\n      discord_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      telegram_chat_id INTEGER,\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n      listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n      listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      rated_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n      score INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(score >= 1 AND score <= 5),\n      comment TEXT DEFAULT '',\n      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n      UNIQUE(listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (rater_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n      FOREIGN KEY (rated_user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    );\n  `);\n  return db;\n});\n\nconst mockSession = vi.hoisted(() => ({\n  userId: 1 as number | undefined,\n}));\n\nconst mockRequireAuth = vi.hoisted(() => vi.fn());\n\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/db\", () => ({ default: testDb }));\nvi.mock(\"@/lib/session\", () => ({ requireAuth: mockRequireAuth }));\n\nconst { GET } = await import(\"@/app/api/profile/reputation/route\");\n\ninterface ReputationData {\n  reputation: {\n    averageScore: number;\n    totalRatings: number;\n    completedGroups: number;\n    groupsRated: number;\n    breakdown: Record<number, number>;\n    recentRatings: Array<{\n      score: number;\n      created_at: string;\n      book_title: string;\n      comment?: string;\n    }>;\n  };\n}\n\ndescribe(\"GET /api/profile/reputation\", () => {\n  let user2Id: number;\n  let listingId: number;\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    vi.clearAllMocks();\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM ratings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listing_members\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM listings\");\n    testDb.exec(\"DELETE FROM users\");\n\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO users (id, email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(1, \"user1@test.com\", \"hash\", \"User One\");\n\n    const result = testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO users (email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(\"user2@test.com\", \"hash\", \"User Two\");\n    user2Id = Number(result.lastInsertRowid);\n\n    const listingResult = testDb.prepare(\n      `INSERT INTO listings (author_id, book_title, book_author, reading_pace, start_date, meeting_format, max_group_size, is_full)\n       VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`\n    ).run(1, \"Test Book\", \"Author\", \"1 ch/week\", \"2026-01-01\", \"text\", 5, 1);\n    listingId = Number(listingResult.lastInsertRowid);\n\n    testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\").run(listingId, 1);\n    testDb.prepare(\"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\").run(listingId, user2Id);\n\n    mockSession.userId = 1;\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 401 when not authenticated\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(null);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    expect(res.status).toBe(401);\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns zero reputation for user with no ratings\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<ReputationData>(res);\n    expect(data.reputation.averageScore).toBe(0);\n    expect(data.reputation.totalRatings).toBe(0);\n    expect(data.reputation.recentRatings).toEqual([]);\n  });\n\n  it(\"calculates average score and breakdown correctly\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n\n    // User2 rates User1 with score 5\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO ratings (listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id, score, comment) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(listingId, user2Id, 1, 5, \"Great reader!\");\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<ReputationData>(res);\n    expect(data.reputation.averageScore).toBe(5);\n    expect(data.reputation.totalRatings).toBe(1);\n    expect(data.reputation.breakdown[5]).toBe(1);\n    expect(data.reputation.breakdown[4]).toBe(0);\n  });\n\n  it(\"counts completed groups correctly\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<ReputationData>(res);\n    // User 1 is member of 1 full listing\n    expect(data.reputation.completedGroups).toBe(1);\n  });\n\n  it(\"includes comments for own profile\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO ratings (listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id, score, comment) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(listingId, user2Id, 1, 4, \"Insightful discussions\");\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<ReputationData>(res);\n    expect(data.reputation.recentRatings[0].comment).toBe(\"Insightful discussions\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"strips comments when viewing another user's profile\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    // Rate user2\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO ratings (listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id, score, comment) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(listingId, 1, user2Id, 3, \"Secret feedback\");\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(`/api/profile/reputation?userId=${user2Id}`);\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<ReputationData>(res);\n    expect(data.reputation.recentRatings[0]).not.toHaveProperty(\"comment\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"returns 400 for invalid userId parameter\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation?userId=abc\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    expect(res.status).toBe(400);\n  });\n\n  it(\"counts groups rated by user\", async () => {\n    mockRequireAuth.mockResolvedValue(mockSession);\n    // User1 rates User2\n    testDb.prepare(\n      \"INSERT INTO ratings (listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id, score) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n    ).run(listingId, 1, user2Id, 4);\n\n    const req = createTestRequest(\"/api/profile/reputation\");\n    const res = await GET(req);\n    const { data } = await parseResponse<ReputationData>(res);\n    expect(data.reputation.groupsRated).toBe(1);\n  });\n});\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":196,"oldLines":6,"newStart":196,"newLines":20,"lines":["     expect(res.status).toBe(400);","   });"," ","+  it(\"returns 400 for userId=0\", async () => {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XSJxFhJEBQec4tTqPkhNc4","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reputation/route.ts has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"16fbd8a9-e0ca-41ad-9a50-7bacd94ee2a5","timestamp":"2026-03-24T15:08:05.602Z","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reputation/route.ts","oldString":"  if (userIdParam && (isNaN(userId as number) || userId < 1)) {","newString":"  if (userIdParam && (isNaN(userId as number) || (userId as number) < 1)) {","originalFile":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\n/**\n * GET /api/profile/reputation?userId=X\n *\n * Returns a user's reputation score and rating breakdown.\n * If no userId is provided, returns the current user's reputation.\n */\nexport async function GET(req: NextRequest) {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n      { status: 401 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const userIdParam = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"userId\");\n  const userId = userIdParam ? parseInt(userIdParam, 10) : session.userId;\n  const isOwnProfile = userId === session.userId;\n\n  if (userIdParam && (isNaN(userId as number) || userId < 1)) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Invalid userId parameter\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Average score\n  const stats = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT\n        COUNT(*) as total_ratings,\n        ROUND(AVG(score), 1) as average_score,\n        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as five_star,\n        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as four_star,\n        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as three_star,\n        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as two_star,\n        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as one_star\n       FROM ratings\n       WHERE rated_user_id = ?`\n    )\n    .get(userId) as {\n      total_ratings: number;\n      average_score: number | null;\n      five_star: number;\n      four_star: number;\n      three_star: number;\n      two_star: number;\n      one_star: number;\n    };\n\n  // Number of completed groups\n  const completedGroups = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT COUNT(*) as count\n       FROM listing_members lm\n       JOIN listings l ON lm.listing_id = l.id\n       WHERE lm.user_id = ? AND l.is_full = 1`\n    )\n    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n\n  // Number of groups where this user has given ratings\n  const groupsRated = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT listing_id) as count\n       FROM ratings\n       WHERE rater_id = ?`\n    )\n    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n\n  // Recent ratings received (with anonymized feedback)\n  const recentRatings = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT r.score, r.comment, r.created_at, l.book_title\n       FROM ratings r\n       JOIN listings l ON r.listing_id = l.id\n       WHERE r.rated_user_id = ?\n       ORDER BY r.created_at DESC\n       LIMIT 10`\n    )\n    .all(userId) as {\n      score: number;\n      comment: string;\n      created_at: string;\n      book_title: string;\n    }[];\n\n  // Strip comments from ratings when viewing other users' profiles (privacy)\n  const sanitizedRatings = isOwnProfile\n    ? recentRatings\n    : recentRatings.map(({ score, created_at, book_title }) => ({\n        score,\n        created_at,\n        book_title,\n      }));\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    reputation: {\n      averageScore: stats.average_score || 0,\n      totalRatings: stats.total_ratings,\n      completedGroups: completedGroups.count,\n      groupsRated: groupsRated.count,\n      breakdown: {\n        5: stats.five_star || 0,\n        4: stats.four_star || 0,\n        3: stats.three_star || 0,\n        2: stats.two_star || 0,\n        1: stats.one_star || 0,\n      },\n      recentRatings: sanitizedRatings,\n    },\n  });\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":21,"oldLines":7,"newStart":21,"newLines":7,"lines":["   const userId = userIdParam ? parseInt(userIdParam, 10) : session.userId;","   const isOwnProfile = userId === session.userId;"," ","-  if (userIdParam && (isNaN(userId as number) || userId < 1)) {","+  if (userIdParam && (isNaN(userId as number) || (userId as number) < 1)) {","     return NextResponse.json(","       { error: \"Invalid userId parameter\" },","       { status: 400 }"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_017BbJiUaSW3yZMJTht9yXAo","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01DGacRFmRTDv3TS8k2DkSxp","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue create --title \"Code review round 18: link exposure, reject race, type safety, validation\" --label \"bug,approved\" --body \"## Findings from code review round 18\n\n### CRITICAL\n1. **Listing browse endpoint exposes invite links** — \\`SELECT l.*\\` in \\`GET /api/listings\\` returns \\`telegram_link\\`, \\`discord_link\\`, \\`telegram_chat_id\\`, \\`discord_channel_id\\` to all users, defeating member-only access intent\n2. **Reject path race condition** — Application reject runs outside transaction without re-checking \\`status='pending'\\`, allowing concurrent approve+reject to overwrite an approval\n\n### IMPORTANT  \n3. **Register route type safety** — \\`displayName\\`, \\`email\\`, \\`password\\` not type-checked before string operations, causing 500 on non-string input\n4. **Apply route missing try/catch** — No top-level error handler; DB exceptions propagate as unhandled errors\n5. **Telegram/Discord link validation** — Server accepts bare \\`https://t.me/\\` with no path; stricter regex needed\n6. **Reputation userId validation** — \\`userId=0\\` or negative passes \\`isNaN\\` guard\n7. **fetchRatings re-triggered** — Client re-fetches ratings on every state-changing action instead of once\" 2>&1","description":"Create GitHub issue"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1225,"cache_read_input_tokens":63235,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1225},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"e4009078-36bb-4253-b22d-6b08dd84f0c7"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01CCFkaHKgdZ2dbdPi8GB5BU","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_012aakwgqEWz475D95pxrdYZ","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nfix: link exposure, reject race, type safety, validation gaps (#110)\n\nCode review round 18 — 7 fixes:\n\n1. Listing browse no longer exposes telegram_link/discord_link (SELECT explicit cols)\n2. Reject path uses WHERE status='pending' to prevent concurrent approve overwrite\n3. Register route validates field types before string operations\n4. Apply route wrapped in try/catch for clean error handling\n5. Telegram/Discord link validation uses strict regex (requires path after domain)\n6. Reputation endpoint rejects userId=0 and negative values\n7. fetchRatings only fires once via ref guard (prevents redundant API calls)\n\n207 tests pass (5 new), ESLint clean, build clean.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\"","description":"Commit changes"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":390,"cache_read_input_tokens":65602,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":390},"output_tokens":47,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"82225933-f359-407d-8486-39ef64f9aa14"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01FfXFvvWENbMJh9yHbf9FAk","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_014EJs1ZKenUQDLJbHX3ZBga","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh pr create --title \"fix: link exposure, reject race, type safety, validation (#110)\" --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Summary\n- **Listing browse endpoint** no longer exposes `telegram_link`, `discord_link`, `telegram_chat_id`, `discord_channel_id` — replaced `SELECT l.*` with explicit column list\n- **Application reject** now uses `WHERE status='pending'` to prevent a concurrent approve from being silently overwritten by a reject\n- **Register route** validates `email`, `password`, `displayName` are strings before calling `.length`/`.trim()` (was returning 500 on non-string input)\n- **Apply route** wrapped in top-level try/catch for clean 500 responses on unexpected DB errors\n- **Telegram/Discord link validation** uses strict regex requiring a path after the domain (rejects bare `https://t.me/`)\n- **Reputation endpoint** rejects `userId=0` and negative values\n- **fetchRatings** only fires once per page load via ref guard (prevents redundant API calls on every action)\n\n## Test plan\n- [x] 207 tests pass (5 new: register type check, listings link exposure, reputation userId bounds)\n- [x] ESLint clean\n- [x] Build clean\n- [x] Verified `GET /api/listings` response no longer contains invite link fields\n\nCloses #110\n\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\nEOF\n)\" 2>&1","description":"Create pull request"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":305,"cache_read_input_tokens":66333,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":305},"output_tokens":64,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"ab491103-da28-4ec1-afe7-2cc517f587fd"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01JudSS1npv18cXSddfEZJxc","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01DptNDbET48wabmoejyDE9q","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-24-1500.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-24 15:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 18: fixed 7 issues (invite link exposure, reject race condition, type safety, error handling, validation). Created issue #110, implemented fixes in PR #111, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production. Verified invite links no longer leak in listing browse API.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 0 restarts\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n- **PM2 logs**: Server action probe errors all from before previous deploy (middleware fix working)\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #110: Code review round 18 (PR #111 — merged + deployed)\n\n7 fixes:\n\n1. **Listing browse exposes invite links** (CRITICAL) — `SELECT l.*` in `GET /api/listings` returned `telegram_link`, `discord_link`, `telegram_chat_id`, `discord_channel_id` to all users. Replaced with explicit column list. Verified in production: fields no longer present.\n\n2. **Reject path race condition** (CRITICAL) — Application reject ran outside transaction without `WHERE status='pending'`, allowing concurrent approve+reject to overwrite an approval. Fixed with conditional UPDATE + 409 on already-decided.\n\n3. **Register route type safety** (IMPORTANT) — `displayName`, `email`, `password` not type-checked before `.length`/`.trim()`, causing 500 on non-string input. Added `typeof` guards.\n\n4. **Apply route missing try/catch** (IMPORTANT) — No top-level error handler; DB exceptions propagated as unhandled Next.js errors. Wrapped in try/catch with clean 500 response.\n\n5. **Telegram/Discord link validation** (IMPORTANT) — Server accepted bare `https://t.me/` with no path. Replaced `startsWith` with strict regex requiring path characters after domain.\n\n6. **Reputation userId validation** (IMPORTANT) — `userId=0` or negative passed `isNaN` guard, returning misleading zeroed stats. Added `< 1` check.\n\n7. **fetchRatings re-triggered** (IMPORTANT) — Client re-fetched ratings on every `setListing` call. Added `ratingsFetchedRef` guard to fire only once.\n\nAll 207 tests pass (5 new), ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human, ~3 days remaining\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider adding tests for untested routes (auth/me, auth/logout, applications, apply)\n4. Performance optimization or new feature proposals\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":499,"cache_read_input_tokens":71090,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":499},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"d4a54cd6-ae8f-4d4f-91a3-71844212c8af"}

Created issue #110, implemented fixes in PR #111, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production. Verified invite links no longer leak in listing browse API.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 0 restarts\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n- **PM2 logs**: Server action probe errors all from before previous deploy (middleware fix working)\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #110: Code review round 18 (PR #111 — merged + deployed)\n\n7 fixes:\n\n1. **Listing browse exposes invite links** (CRITICAL) — `SELECT l.*` in `GET /api/listings` returned `telegram_link`, `discord_link`, `telegram_chat_id`, `discord_channel_id` to all users. Replaced with explicit column list. Verified in production: fields no longer present.\n\n2. **Reject path race condition** (CRITICAL) — Application reject ran outside transaction without `WHERE status='pending'`, allowing concurrent approve+reject to overwrite an approval. Fixed with conditional UPDATE + 409 on already-decided.\n\n3. **Register route type safety** (IMPORTANT) — `displayName`, `email`, `password` not type-checked before `.length`/`.trim()`, causing 500 on non-string input. Added `typeof` guards.\n\n4. **Apply route missing try/catch** (IMPORTANT) — No top-level error handler; DB exceptions propagated as unhandled Next.js errors. Wrapped in try/catch with clean 500 response.\n\n5. **Telegram/Discord link validation** (IMPORTANT) — Server accepted bare `https://t.me/` with no path. Replaced `startsWith` with strict regex requiring path characters after domain.\n\n6. **Reputation userId validation** (IMPORTANT) — `userId=0` or negative passed `isNaN` guard, returning misleading zeroed stats. Added `< 1` check.\n\n7. **fetchRatings re-triggered** (IMPORTANT) — Client re-fetched ratings on every `setListing` call. Added `ratingsFetchedRef` guard to fire only once.\n\nAll 207 tests pass (5 new), ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: Critical — needs-human, ~3 days remaining\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider adding tests for untested routes (auth/me, auth/logout, applications, apply)\n4. Performance optimization or new feature proposals\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01CenDGgoHZ7eWzLfL5cVF9n","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Session complete. Here's what I accomplished:\n\n## Session Summary — 2026-03-24 15:00 UTC\n\n**Code review round 18** — found and fixed 7 issues across the codebase:\n\n### Critical Fixes\n1. **Invite link data exposure** — `GET /api/listings` was returning `telegram_link` and `discord_link` to all users via `SELECT l.*`. Replaced with explicit column list. Verified in production.\n2. **Application reject race condition** — Reject path could overwrite a concurrent approval. Fixed with `WHERE status='pending'` guard.\n\n### Important Fixes\n3. Register route type safety (non-string fields caused 500)\n4. Apply route missing try/catch (unhandled DB exceptions)\n5. Telegram/Discord link validation (accepted bare domain URLs)\n6. Reputation `userId=0` and negative values accepted\n7. Client-side `fetchRatings` fired redundantly on every action\n\n**Results**: 207 tests pass (5 new), ESLint clean, build clean, CI green, deployed to production. PR #111 merged, issue #110 closed."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":289,"cache_read_input_tokens":72599,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":289},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a6655ab4-4f7a-45c6-916e-a3858cd82d67","uuid":"f6259326-9318-4ee8-80b5-3d7372f85109"}
